1	---
2	name: lean-auditor
3	description: Whole-project read-only audit of all .lean code, with no strategy bias. Per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, bad Lean practices. Critical pushback on excuse-comments ("temporary wrong def", "will fix later") — treats them as red flags, not workflow.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am highly recommended every review phase. The audit warning
11	    fires if I am skipped without a recorded rationale.
12	
13	    **You may skip me this iter when ALL of:**
14	      - no `.lean` file under the project tree was modified this iter
15	        (the prover phase committed no edits — check via
16	        `git diff --stat HEAD~1 -- '*.lean'`);
17	      - my prior verdict had no must-fix-this-iter findings.
18	
19	    Record the skip under `## Subagent skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`.
20	  - Pass me as LITTLE strategic context as possible. Do NOT include
21	    STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, or "this is what we are trying to prove"
22	    framing in the directive. My value depends on auditing the Lean
23	    *without* bias toward what the strategy claims should be true.
24	  - Acceptable directive content: a list of files, optional focus areas
25	    (e.g. "pay extra attention to Algebra/"), and the absolute paths
26	    to read. That's it.
27	  - My output is a per-file checklist plus a flagged-issues block. Use
28	    it to seed `recommendations.md` and (when severe) trigger a
29	    structural-refactor directive (via the catalog's refactor
30	    subagent) next iter.
31	  - I audit the Lean as Lean. The catalog may include a separate
32	    Lean ↔ blueprint checker that compares Lean against the
33	    chapter — run both for full coverage when both are present.
34	---
35	
36	# Lean Auditor
37	
38	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.
39	
40	You are **highly recommended in the review phase**: the review agent dispatches you whenever any `.lean` file has been modified this iter, OR whenever the prior verdict had live must-fix findings. Your output flags issues the prover and plan agents may have missed.
41	
42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
54	
55	## Directive Format
56	
57	```markdown
58	# Lean Auditor Directive
59	
60	## Slug
61	<slug>
62	
63	## Scope (files)
64	<"all" by default. Optionally a list of file paths if the review agent wants to narrow scope; never expand beyond the project.>
65	
66	## Focus areas (optional)
67	<directories or namespaces to pay extra attention to. Bias toward thoroughness — do not skip the rest of the project just because focus is named.>
68	
69	## Known issues
70	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
71	```
72	
73	The directive **does not** include STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, references, or a description of what the project is trying to prove. That is by design.
74	
75	## What you do
76	
77	1. Read your directive.
78	2. **List every `.lean` file** under the project. Use `find` or `ls -R` — do not rely on import graphs (some files may be temporarily orphaned).
79	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
80	   - Read the whole file.
81	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
82	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
83	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
84	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
85	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.
86	
87	You may use:
88	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics. Useful to verify a declaration's actual type.
89	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to get sorry counts per file.
90	- Standard `Read` / `Grep` for code reading.
91	
92	You may NOT modify any project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
93	
94	## Report format
95	
96	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-auditor-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
97	
98	```markdown
99	# Lean Audit Report
100	
101	## Slug
102	<slug>
103	
104	## Iteration
105	<NNN>
106	
107	## Scope
108	- files audited: <N>
109	- files skipped (per directive): <N> — <reason>
110	
111	## Per-file checklist
112	
113	### <path/to/file.lean>
114	- **outdated comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
115	- **suspect definitions**: <N flagged> (or "none")
116	- **dead-end proofs**: <N flagged> (or "none")
117	- **bad practices**: <N flagged> (or "none")
118	- **excuse-comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
119	- **notes**:
120	  - <one bullet per finding, naming line number where relevant>
121	  - ...
122	
123	### <next file>
124	- ...
125	
126	(One block per file. Cover every file, including the ones with no findings — they get `none` everywhere and `notes: -`.)
127	
128	## Must-fix-this-iter
129	
130	Apply verbatim. Every one of the following lands here automatically, no exceptions, no under-classification:
131	
132	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.
133	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).
134	- **Parallel APIs of existing Mathlib**: a project file that copy-and-modifies a Mathlib definition (changing one type-class or argument) instead of instancing the existing Mathlib version. Bridge lemmas needed to consume it are the symptom.
135	- **Suspect bodies on substantive claims**: `:= sorry` on a load-bearing claim, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial statement, `:= Classical.choice _` without authorization.
136	- **Axioms** on non-trivial claims that the project hasn't explicitly authorized in the strategy.
137	
138	A finding that meets any of the above lands at must-fix-this-iter — even if classifying it as "major" would feel more diplomatic. Soft severity is how wrong code hardens into the project.
139	
140	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>. Why must-fix: <one line>.
141	- ...
142	
143	## Major
144	
145	Findings that are real issues but don't meet the must-fix bar — typically stale comments that don't actively mislead, naming drift, minor code smells.
146	
147	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
148	- ...
149	
150	## Minor
151	
152	Low-impact observations.
153	
154	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
155	- ...
156	
157	## Excuse-comments (always called out separately)
158	
159	If any excuse-comments were flagged, list each one verbatim with its file:line. These deserve special visibility because they document the project lying to itself.
160	
161	- `Foo/Bar.lean:42`: "TODO: replace with the real definition" (attached to `Foo.bar`, which is a load-bearing definition in `Baz.lean`). Severity: critical.
162	- ...
163	
164	## Severity summary
165	
166	- **must-fix-this-iter**: <N> — these block downstream work in their files until addressed (see plan.md's per-file gate).
167	- **major**: <N>
168	- **minor**: <N>
169	- **excuse-comments**: <N> (also counted under must-fix-this-iter above; called out separately because they document the project lying to itself).
170	
171	Overall verdict: one sentence.
172	```
173	
174	**Strict severity reminder.** The plan agent's gate treats must-fix-this-iter as blocking. If you under-classify a wrong-decision finding to keep the loop's momentum, you are working against the project — wrong code accumulates faster than reviewers can catch it later. When in doubt, classify higher, not lower.
175	
176	## Return value
177	
178	Your final assistant message:
179	
180	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> files audited, <M> issues (critical/major/minor: a/b/c)`
181	- The path to your full report.
182	
183	## Reminders
184	
185	- **Read every file. No scope shortcuts.**
186	- **No strategic context.** Your audit is unbiased by design.
187	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
188	- **Excuse-comments are red flags.** Critical or major severity, every time.
189	- **Per-file checklist is the primary output.** Cover every file; the format is fixed.
190

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
153

9	# Auslander–Buchsbaum formula (A.4.b)
10	
11	This file is the **A.4.b** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
13	Auslander–Buchsbaum formula and the corollary "regular local ring
14	⟹ Cohen–Macaulay" that the sibling sub-build **A.4.a** (codim-1 extension of a
15	rational map across a codim-2 closed point on a regular projective surface)
16	consumes.
17	
18	Per STRATEGY.md L30 this row is gated downstream on A.4.a but is independently
19	startable on the Mathlib side: the algebra here is decoupled from the Albanese
20	geometry and lives entirely under `RingTheory.*`. Mathlib at the project's
21	pinned commit (`b80f227`) exposes `IsRegularLocalRing` and the categorical
22	`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension` on an abelian category (specialised to
23	`ModuleCat R`), as well as the regular-sequence definition
24	`RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular`. The depth function `Module.depth`, the
25	Auslander–Buchsbaum formula, and the Cohen–Macaulay predicate are *not* in
26	Mathlib at the pinned commit — they are the new content scaffolded here.
27	
28	## Status (iter-178 update — projectiveDimension closed)
29

31	blueprint-pinned declaration carrying the *intended* substantive type
32	signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in

42	`CohenMacaulay.of_regular`) are substantive multi-iter content and stay

47	1. `RingTheory.Module.depth` (noncomputable def, ~6 LOC) — the `I`-depth of a
48	   finite `R`-module as the supremum of lengths of `M`-regular sequences in

54	3. `Module.projectiveDimension` (noncomputable def, ~3 LOC) — an

69	## Note on type expressivity
70	
71	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
72	declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
73	
74	- `depth` returns `ℕ∞` and is defined by the regular-sequence supremum.
75	- `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` is encoded as the depth-bound `↔` `Ext`
76	  vanishing characterisation (the smallest non-vanishing `Ext` index = depth).
77	- `projectiveDimension` re-exports the categorical

79	- `depth_of_short_exact` packages the three Stacks 00LE inequalities into a

84	- `CohenMacaulay.of_regular` is an `IsRegularLocalRing → IsCohenMacaulayLocalRing`

112	/-! ## §1. Depth of a finite module
113	
114	The `I`-depth of a finite `R`-module `M` is the supremum in
115	`{0, 1, 2, …, ∞}` of the lengths of `M`-regular sequences contained in `I`
116	(provided `IM ≠ M`; if `IM = M` we set `depth_I(M) = ∞`). Mathlib `b80f227`
117	exposes the regular-sequence predicate `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular`
118	(`Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.RegularSequence`) but not the resulting
119	numeric depth function — that is the gap this declaration fills.
120	
121	Blueprint reference: `def:depth` (Stacks tag 00LF). -/
122	
123	/-- The **`I`-depth** of a finite `R`-module `M`: the supremum (in `ℕ∞`) of
124	lengths of `M`-regular sequences contained in the ideal `I`.
125	
126	When `IM = M` (the "trivial-quotient" case, e.g. `M = 0` or `I = R`) the
127	supremum is taken to be `⊤` by convention. When `(R, 𝔪)` is local one usually
128	calls `depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M` simply *the depth* of `M`.
129	
130	iter-176+: the body is the supremum
131	```
132	sSup { (n : ℕ∞) | ∃ rs : List R, rs.length = n ∧ (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ I) ∧
133	                  RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs }
134	```
135	folded with the `IM = M` clause. The signature is non-tautological: it
136	asserts a function `(Ideal R) → (M : Type v) → ℕ∞` matching the Stacks
137	00LF definition.
138	
139	**iter-179 Mathlib-gap check (Lane F Target 2 STRETCH)**: a pinned-commit
140	audit of `Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.Depth` (the only file under
141	`Mathlib/RingTheory/*` containing the word "depth") confirms that Mathlib
142	`b80f227` ships only depth-zero lemmas (`IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff`)
143	and *not* the numeric depth function itself. The one-liner re-export route
144	is therefore not available; the body stays a typed `sorry` until an
145	iter-180+ body lane fills the supremum-with-`IM=M` clause directly. -/
146	noncomputable def depth {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (_I : Ideal R)
147	    (_M : Type v) [AddCommGroup _M] [Module R _M] : ℕ∞ :=
148	  open Classical in
149	  if _I • (⊤ : Submodule R _M) = ⊤ then (⊤ : ℕ∞)
150	  else sSup { n : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = n ∧
151	    (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ _I) ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular _M rs }
152	
153	end Module
154	
155	end RingTheory

157	/-! ## §2. Projective dimension
158	
159	Mathlib `b80f227` exposes the categorical
160	`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension : C → WithBot ℕ∞` on an abelian category
161	with enough projectives (file `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.Projective.Dimension`).
162	For `R`-modules this specialises to `ModuleCat.of R M`. The blueprint pins the
163	`Module.projectiveDimension` name as the re-export that downstream consumers
164	can use directly on an `R`-module without first packaging it in `ModuleCat`.
165	
166	Blueprint reference: `def:projective_dimension`. -/
167	
168	namespace Module
169	
170	/-- The **projective dimension** of an `R`-module `M`, defined as the
171	categorical projective dimension of `ModuleCat.of R M`.
172	
173	This is a re-export of `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension` specialised to
174	the abelian category `ModuleCat R`. The categorical definition is the
175	infimum (in `WithBot ℕ∞`) of `n : ℕ` such that all `Ext^i(M, -)` vanish for
176	`i > n`, equivalently the smallest length of a projective resolution of `M`.
177	
178	Mathlib has the categorical `projectiveDimension` and the module-specific
179	`ModuleCat.projectiveDimension_eq_of_linearEquiv`; the blueprint pins the
180	wrapper name so downstream consumers can write
181	`Module.projectiveDimension R M` rather than threading `ModuleCat.of`.
182	
183	The body is the one-line re-export
184	`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R _M)`; iter-178 closed
185	this declaration kernel-clean. -/
186	noncomputable def projectiveDimension (R : Type u) [Ring R]
187	    (_M : Type u) [AddCommGroup _M] [Module R _M] : WithBot ℕ∞ :=
188	  CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R _M)
189	
190	end Module

1133	/-- **The Auslander–Buchsbaum formula.** Let `(R, 𝔪)` be a Noetherian local
1134	ring and let `M` be a nonzero finite `R`-module of finite projective

1139

1151	theorem auslander_buchsbaum_formula
1152	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1153	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
1154	    [Nontrivial M]
1155	    (n : ℕ)
1156	    (_hpd : _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R M = (n : WithBot ℕ∞)) :
1157	    (n : ℕ∞) + Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
1158	      = Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R := by
1159	  -- iter-193 Lane G: structural scaffold for Stacks 090V. We split on `n` to
1160	  -- isolate the base case `pd_R(M) = 0` (where `M` is finite free over a
1161	  -- Noetherian local ring) from the inductive step `pd_R(M) = k + 1` (which
1162	  -- inducts on `depth(M)` via the snake-lemma-on-minimal-resolution recipe).
1163	  --
1164	  -- The `depth_eq_of_linearEquiv` helper above (axiom-clean) is the substrate
1165	  -- piece used in the `n = 0` case to identify `depth(M)` with `depth(R^k)`
1166	  -- for `M ≃ₗ[R] (Fin k → R)`; the residual `depth(R^k) = depth(R)` step is
1167	  -- the remaining substrate gap (a single direct-sum-of-modules depth fact).
1168	  rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos n with hn0 | hn_pos
1169	  · -- **Base case `n = 0`**: `pd_R(M) = 0` ⟹ `M` projective ⟹ (finite + local)
1170	    -- `M` free ⟹ `depth(M) = depth(R)`. The formula
1171	    -- `0 + depth(M) = depth(R)` collapses to `depth(M) = depth(R)`.
1172	    subst hn0
1173	    rw [Nat.cast_zero, zero_add]
1174	    -- Step 1: unfold `_root_.Module.projectiveDimension` to the categorical
1175	    -- form, then apply `projectiveDimension_eq_zero_iff`.
1176	    have hpd' :
1177	        CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R M) = (0 : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
1178	      unfold _root_.Module.projectiveDimension at _hpd
1179	      exact _hpd
1180	    obtain ⟨hproj, _hNonZero⟩ :=
1181	      (CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension_eq_zero_iff _).mp hpd'
1182	    -- Step 2: `CategoryTheory.Projective (ModuleCat.of R M)` ⟹ `Module.Projective R M`.
1183	    have hMproj : _root_.Module.Projective R M :=
1184	      (IsProjective.iff_projective M).mpr hproj

1191	    -- axiom-clean `depth_eq_of_linearEquiv` helper. The residual is the
1192	    -- single, narrower statement: `depth(Fin k → R) = depth(R)` for `k ≥ 1`.

1194	      (_root_.Module.finrank_pos_iff_of_free R M).mpr inferInstance
1195	    set k : ℕ := _root_.Module.finrank R M with hk_def
1196	    -- Build the equivalence `M ≃ₗ[R] (Fin k → R)` via the chosen basis.
1197	    let e : M ≃ₗ[R] (Fin k → R) := (_root_.Module.finBasis R M).equivFun
1198	    -- Transport `depth(M) = depth(Fin k → R)` using the axiom-clean helper.
1199	    have hdepth_M_eq : Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
1200	        = Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (Fin k → R) :=
1201	      Module.depth_eq_of_linearEquiv _ e
1202	    rw [hdepth_M_eq]

1204	    -- `Module.depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty` which proves the substrate
1205	    -- `depth(ι → M) = depth(M)` for nonempty finite `ι` (Pi-quotient
1206	    -- decomposition + regular-sequence transport).
1207	    haveI : Nonempty (Fin k) := ⟨⟨0, hk⟩⟩
1208	    exact Module.depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty _
1209	  · -- **Inductive step `n = k + 1`**: delegate to the iter-195 Lane G
1210	    -- structural-carving helper `auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd`,

1217	/-! ## §6. Cohen–Macaulay local rings
1218	
1219	A Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` is **Cohen–Macaulay** if its depth equals
1220	its Krull dimension (Stacks tag 00N4). Mathlib `b80f227` has neither the
1221	predicate nor the class — this file is the upstream gap-fill.
1222	
1223	Blueprint reference: `def:cohen_macaulay_local` (Stacks tag 00N4). -/
1224	
1225	/-- A Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` is **Cohen–Macaulay** if its depth
1226	equals its Krull dimension: `depth(R) = dim R`.
1227	
1228	Encoded as a `Prop`-valued type class so downstream consumers can write
1229	`[CohenMacaulay R]` and use Cohen–Macaulay as a hypothesis. Mathlib at the
1230	pinned commit (`b80f227`) does not expose any Cohen–Macaulay predicate;
1231	this is the upstream gap-fill.
1232	
1233	iter-176+: the predicate is `Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R =
1234	ringKrullDim R`. For the iter-175 file-skeleton the carrier definition is a
1235	typed `sorry` at the `Prop` level — substantively, the predicate is the
1236	named equality, but we package it as a `class` so use sites are uniform. -/
1237	class CohenMacaulay (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
1238	    [IsNoetherianRing R] : Prop where
1239	  /-- The Cohen–Macaulay equation: `depth(R) = ringKrullDim R`. The numeric
1240	  comparison is in `WithBot ℕ∞` after coercion of the `ℕ∞`-valued depth. -/
1241	  depth_eq_krullDim :
1242	    (Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R : WithBot ℕ∞) = ringKrullDim R
1243	
1244	/-! ## §7. Regular local rings are Cohen–Macaulay
1245	
1246	The consumer-facing input for A.4.a: every regular Noetherian local ring is
1247	Cohen–Macaulay (Stacks tag 00OD). The direct proof: pick a minimal
1248	generating set `x_1, …, x_d` of `𝔪` (where `d = dim R`), use that `R` is a
1249	domain (Stacks 00NQ) to start an `R`-regular sequence, and induct on
1250	dimension — each `R/(x_1, …, x_c)` is again regular of dimension `d - c`,
1251	so `x_1, …, x_d` is an `R`-regular sequence and `depth(R) ≥ d`. The reverse
1252	inequality `depth(R) ≤ dim R` is the standard depth bound (Stacks 00LK).
1253	
1254	Blueprint reference: `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` (Stacks tag 00OD). -/
1255	
1256	namespace CohenMacaulay
1257	
1258	/-! ### Helper 1 (axiom-clean): length-bound on regular sequences
1259	
1260	For a Noetherian local ring `R`, every `R`-regular sequence has length at most
1261	`ringKrullDim R`. This is the **upper bound** half of Stacks 00OD: it is the
1262	specialisation of the equality
1263	`ringKrullDim (R / ofList rs) + rs.length = ringKrullDim R`
1264	(`ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular`) to the observation that
1265	`ringKrullDim (R / ofList rs) ≥ 0` whenever the quotient is nontrivial, which it
1266	is precisely because `IsRegular` rules out `rs • ⊤ = ⊤`.
1267	
1268	iter-181 Lane G: closed kernel-clean. -/
1269	private lemma length_le_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular

216	/-! ### Helper C (iter-183 Lane G, axiom-clean): `Ann`-killing of Ext via R-linearity
217	
218	For any `R`-modules `N, M` and any `x : R` in the annihilator of `N`, the
219	R-action `x • e` on `e : Ext^i_R(N, M)` is zero.
220	
221	Proof sketch: `x • e = (mk₀ (x • 𝟙_N)).comp e (zero_add i)` (by R-linearity:
222	`mk₀_smul + smul_comp + mk₀_id_comp`). For `x ∈ Ann(N)` the morphism
223	`x • 𝟙_N : N ⟶ N` is the zero map, so `mk₀ (x • 𝟙_N) = mk₀ 0 = 0`
224	(`mk₀_zero`), and `0.comp e = 0` (`zero_comp`).
225	
226	iter-183 Lane G: closed kernel-clean. This is the precise statement of the
227	Stacks-00LP "`x ∈ 𝔪` annihilates `Ext^*(κ, -)`" trick, lifted to the more
228	general `x ∈ Ann(N)` form so it covers both `N = κ` and `N = R/(x_1,…,x_k)`. -/
229	private lemma ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator
230	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
231	    {N M : ModuleCat.{u} R} {i : ℕ} (e : Abelian.Ext.{u} N M i)
232	    {x : R} (hx : x ∈ Module.annihilator R (N : Type u)) :
233	    x • e = 0 := by
234	  -- Step 1: x • 𝟙_N = 0 in ModuleCat (the underlying linear map sends m ↦ x • m,
235	  -- which is 0 since x ∈ Ann(N)).
236	  have hkill : (x • (𝟙 N : N ⟶ N)) = (0 : N ⟶ N) := by
237	    apply ModuleCat.hom_ext
238	    apply LinearMap.ext
239	    intro n
240	    change x • n = 0
241	    exact Module.mem_annihilator.mp hx n
242	  -- Step 2: x • e = (mk₀ (x • 𝟙_N)).comp e (zero_add i) by R-linearity.
243	  have hreflect :
244	      (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (x • (𝟙 N : N ⟶ N))).comp e (zero_add i)
245	        = x • e := by
246	    have hmk : (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (x • (𝟙 N : N ⟶ N))
247	                : Abelian.Ext.{u} N N 0)
248	        = x • CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (𝟙 N) :=
249	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_smul (R := R) x (𝟙 N)
250	    rw [hmk, CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.smul_comp,
251	        CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp]
252	  -- Step 3: substitute hkill to collapse mk₀ … to mk₀ 0 = 0, then zero_comp.
253	  rw [← hreflect, hkill, CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_zero,
254	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.zero_comp]

258	depth-bound `n ≤ depth(M)` is equivalent to the vanishing of `Ext^i_R(κ, M)`
259	for all `i < n`. Equivalently, `depth(M)` is the smallest `i` at which
260	`Ext^i_R(κ, M)` is nonzero.

517	        IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff.mp hsubsing
518	      have hxMem : x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := hannih ▸ hxAnnih
519	      -- Step 3: Pass to `M/xM := QuotSMulTop x M` and apply IH at index n.
520	      -- iter-184+: the remaining LES chase to derive Ext-vanishing on M/xM,
521	      -- application of IH, and `isRegular_cons_iff` assembly closes the rest.
522	      -- We package the partial state in named witnesses for the next iter.
523	      let MxM : Type u := QuotSMulTop x M
524	      haveI : Nontrivial MxM :=
525	        nontrivial_quotSMulTop_of_mem_maximalIdeal M hxMem
526	      -- `Module.Finite R (M / xM)` is automatic via `Module.Finite.quotient`.
527	      haveI : _root_.Module.Finite R MxM := inferInstance
528	      -- iter-184 Lane G: closed via LES chase + sSup extraction + cons.
529	      --
530	      -- Step A: derive `∀ j < n, Ext^j(κ, MxM) = 0` from `hext` via the
531	      --   LES of `Ext^*(κ, ·)` on the SES `0 → M →[x] M → MxM → 0`.
532	      let S : ShortComplex (ModuleCat.{u} R) :=
533	        ModuleCat.smulShortComplex (ModuleCat.of R M) x
534	      have hS : S.ShortExact := hxReg.smulShortComplex_shortExact
535	      set κ : ModuleCat.{u} R := ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)
536	        with hκ
537	      have hMxM_vanish : ∀ j < n, ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u} κ
538	          (ModuleCat.of R MxM) j, e = 0 := by
539	        intro j hj e
540	        -- `e.comp hS.extClass rfl : Ext κ M (j+1) = 0` by `hext` at `j+1`.
541	        have he_ext : e.comp hS.extClass (rfl : j + 1 = j + 1) = 0 :=
542	          hext (j + 1) (by omega) _
543	        obtain ⟨x₂, hx₂⟩ :=
544	          CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₃ κ hS e rfl he_ext
545	        -- `x₂ : Ext κ M j = 0` by `hext` at `j`.
546	        have hx₂_zero : x₂ = 0 := hext j (by omega) _
547	        rw [hx₂_zero] at hx₂
548	        simpa using hx₂.symm
549	      -- Step B: apply `ih (M := MxM)` at index `n`.
550	      have hdepth_MxM : (n : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) MxM :=
551	        (ih (M := MxM)).mpr hMxM_vanish
552	      -- Step C: unfold `depth MxM = sSup`-clause and extract a witness rs'
553	      --   of length ≥ n on MxM in 𝔪 (when n ≥ 1; the n = 0 case uses []).
554	      have hne_MxM : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R MxM) ≠ ⊤ :=
555	        Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
556	          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
557	      have hdepth_MxM_unfold :
558	          depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) MxM
559	            = sSup { k : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = k ∧
560	              (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
561	              ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM rs } := by
562	        rw [depth, if_neg hne_MxM]
563	      obtain ⟨rs', hrs'_len_ge, hrs'_mem, hrs'_reg⟩ :
564	          ∃ rs' : List R, n ≤ rs'.length ∧
565	            (∀ r ∈ rs', r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ∧
566	            RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM rs' := by
567	        rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos n with hn0 | hn_pos
568	        · subst hn0
569	          exact ⟨[], by simp, by simp,
570	            RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.nil R MxM⟩
571	        · have hlt : ((n - 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) <
572	              depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) MxM := by
573	            have h1 : ((n - 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) < (n : ℕ∞) := by
574	              exact_mod_cast Nat.sub_lt hn_pos Nat.one_pos
575	            exact h1.trans_le hdepth_MxM
576	          rw [hdepth_MxM_unfold] at hlt
577	          obtain ⟨k, ⟨rs', hrs'_len_eq, hrs'_mem, hrs'_reg⟩, hk_lt⟩ :=
578	            lt_sSup_iff.mp hlt
579	          have hrs'_len_gt : n - 1 < rs'.length := by
580	            rw [← hrs'_len_eq] at hk_lt
581	            exact_mod_cast hk_lt
582	          refine ⟨rs', ?_, hrs'_mem, hrs'_reg⟩
583	          omega
584	      -- Step D: truncate rs' to length n; resulting sequence is M-regular.
585	      let rs_n := rs'.take n
586	      have hrs_n_len : rs_n.length = n := by
587	        change (rs'.take n).length = n
588	        rw [List.length_take]
589	        omega
590	      have hrs_n_mem : ∀ r ∈ rs_n, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := fun r hr =>
591	        hrs'_mem r (List.mem_of_mem_take hr)
592	      have hrs_n_reg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM rs_n := by
593	        change RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM (rs'.take n)
594	        have hsplit : rs' = rs'.take n ++ rs'.drop n :=
595	          (List.take_append_drop _ _).symm
596	        have hwr : RingTheory.Sequence.IsWeaklyRegular MxM rs' :=
597	          hrs'_reg.toIsWeaklyRegular
598	        rw [hsplit] at hwr
599	        have hwr_n : RingTheory.Sequence.IsWeaklyRegular MxM (rs'.take n) :=

600	          ((RingTheory.Sequence.isWeaklyRegular_append_iff MxM _ _).mp hwr).1
601	        exact (IsLocalRing.isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_maximalIdeal
602	          hrs_n_mem).mpr hwr_n
603	      -- Step E: cons x to form a length-(n+1) M-regular sequence in 𝔪.
604	      have hrs_M_reg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M (x :: rs_n) :=
605	        (RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff M x rs_n).mpr ⟨hxReg, hrs_n_reg⟩
606	      have hrs_M_mem : ∀ r ∈ (x :: rs_n), r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
607	        intro r hr
608	        rcases List.mem_cons.mp hr with rfl | hr
609	        · exact hxMem
610	        · exact hrs_n_mem r hr
611	      have hrs_M_len : (x :: rs_n).length = n + 1 := by simp [hrs_n_len]
612	      -- Step F: conclude `(n+1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M` via `le_sSup`.
613	      have hne_M : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R M) ≠ ⊤ :=
614	        Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
615	          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
616	      rw [depth, if_neg hne_M]
617	      apply le_sSup
618	      refine ⟨x :: rs_n, ?_, hrs_M_mem, hrs_M_reg⟩
619	      exact_mod_cast hrs_M_len

621	/-! ## §4. Depth on a short exact sequence
622	
623	For a short exact sequence `0 → N' → N → N'' → 0` of nonzero finite modules
624	over a Noetherian local ring, the three modules' depths satisfy three
625	crosswise inequalities (Stacks tag 00LE), each a direct read-off of the
626	long exact `Ext^*(κ, -)` sequence and the depth-via-Ext characterisation
627	of §3.
628	
629	Blueprint reference: `lem:depth_short_exact_sequence` (Stacks tag 00LE). -/
630	
631	/-! ### Helper A (iter-182 Lane G, axiom-clean): Ext-vanishing from strict depth bound
632	
633	For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` and a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`,
634	if `(i : ℕ∞) < depth M` then every element of `Ext^i_R(κ, M)` is zero.
635	
636	This packages `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` for the LES chase: the
637	`n ≤ depth M` form with `n := i + 1` instantiates the `∀ j < i + 1`
638	quantifier at `j = i`. Body is kernel-clean modulo the typed sorry of
639	`depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`. -/
640	private lemma ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth
641	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
642	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
643	    [Nontrivial M] {i : ℕ}
644	    (h : (i : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M)
645	    (e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.{u}
646	        (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
647	        (ModuleCat.of R M) i) : e = 0 := by
648	  have h' : ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := by
649	    have hcast : ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = (i : ℕ∞) + 1 := by push_cast; ring
650	    rw [hcast]; exact Order.add_one_le_of_lt h
651	  exact (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := M) (i + 1)).mp h' i (Nat.lt_succ_self i) e
652	
653	/-! ### Helper B (iter-182 Lane G, axiom-clean): `ℕ∞` tsub bridge
654	
655	If `(a : ℕ) ≤ d - 1` in `ℕ∞` and `1 ≤ a` (in `ℕ`), then
656	`((a + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ d`.
657	
658	Case-split on `d = ⊤` (trivial) and `d = ↑n` (drop to `ℕ` arithmetic).
659	Used for the `depth N' - 1` shift in the second SES inequality. -/
660	private lemma natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one
661	    {d : ℕ∞} {a : ℕ} (ha : 1 ≤ a) (h : (a : ℕ∞) ≤ d - 1) :
662	    ((a + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ d := by
663	  rcases eq_or_ne d ⊤ with hd | hd
664	  · simp [hd]
665	  · obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := WithTop.ne_top_iff_exists.mp hd
666	    -- Reduce to ℕ: turn `↑a ≤ ↑n - 1` into `a ≤ n - 1`, then `a + 1 ≤ n`.
667	    have h₂ : (a : ℕ∞) ≤ ((n - 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) := by
668	      refine h.trans (le_of_eq ?_)
669	      rcases n with _ | n
670	      · rfl
671	      · push_cast; rfl
672	    have han : a ≤ n - 1 := by exact_mod_cast h₂
673	    have hle : a + 1 ≤ n := by omega
674	    exact Nat.cast_le.mpr hle
675	
676	theorem depth_of_short_exact
677	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
678	    {N' N N'' : Type u}
679	    [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N'] [_root_.Module.Finite R N'] [Nontrivial N']
680	    [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [_root_.Module.Finite R N] [Nontrivial N]
681	    [AddCommGroup N''] [Module R N''] [_root_.Module.Finite R N''] [Nontrivial N'']
682	    (f : N' →ₗ[R] N) (g : N →ₗ[R] N'')
683	    (_hf : Function.Injective f) (_hg : Function.Surjective g)
684	    (_hex : Function.Exact f g) :
685	    min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N')
686	        (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'')
687	      ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N
688	    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
689	          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' - 1)
690	        ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N''
691	    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
692	          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' + 1)
693	        ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' := by
694	  -- Package the SES as a `ShortComplex.ShortExact` in `ModuleCat.{u} R`.
695	  let S : ShortComplex (ModuleCat.{u} R) :=
696	    ShortComplex.mk (ModuleCat.ofHom f) (ModuleCat.ofHom g)
697	      (by ext x; simpa using _hex.apply_apply_eq_zero x)
698	  have hS : S.ShortExact :=
699	    ModuleCat.shortComplex_shortExact S _hex _hf _hg
700	  -- The residue field as a ModuleCat object.
701	  set κ : ModuleCat.{u} R := ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) with hκ
702	  refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
703	  · -- (1) min(depth N', depth N'') ≤ depth N
704	    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
705	    intro a ha
706	    rw [le_min_iff] at ha
707	    obtain ⟨haN', haN''⟩ := ha
708	    rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
709	    intro i hi e
710	    -- `e : Ext κ S.X₂ i = Ext κ (of R N) i`; goal `e = 0`.
711	    have hicast : (i : ℕ∞) < (a : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast hi
712	    have hiN' : (i : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' := hicast.trans_le haN'
713	    have hiN'' : (i : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' := hicast.trans_le haN''
714	    -- `e ∘ S.g ∈ Ext κ (of R N'') i = 0`.
715	    have heg : e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.g) (add_zero i) = 0 :=
716	      ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hiN'' _
717	    obtain ⟨x₁, hx₁⟩ :=
718	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₂ κ hS e heg
719	    -- `x₁ ∈ Ext κ (of R N') i = 0`.
720	    have hx₁_zero : x₁ = 0 := ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hiN' _
721	    rw [hx₁_zero] at hx₁
722	    simpa using hx₁.symm
723	  · -- (2) min(depth N, depth N' - 1) ≤ depth N''
724	    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
725	    intro a ha
726	    rw [le_min_iff] at ha
727	    obtain ⟨haN, haN'sub⟩ := ha
728	    rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
729	    intro i hi e
730	    -- `e : Ext κ S.X₃ i = Ext κ (of R N'') i`; goal `e = 0`.
731	    have hicast : (i : ℕ∞) < (a : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast hi
732	    have hiN : (i : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N := hicast.trans_le haN
733	    -- `↑(i+1) < depth N'`: use Helper B with `a` and the inequality `hi : i + 1 ≤ a`.
734	    have hia : 1 ≤ a := by omega
735	    have ha1 : ((a + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' :=
736	      natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one hia haN'sub
737	    have hsucc : ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' := by
738	      have : ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) < ((a + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast Nat.add_lt_add_right hi 1
739	      exact this.trans_le ha1
740	    -- `e ∘ extClass ∈ Ext κ (of R N') (i + 1) = 0`.
741	    have hext : e.comp hS.extClass rfl = 0 :=
742	      ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hsucc _
743	    obtain ⟨x₂, hx₂⟩ :=
744	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₃ κ hS e rfl hext
745	    -- `x₂ ∈ Ext κ (of R N) i = 0`.
746	    have hx₂_zero : x₂ = 0 := ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hiN _
747	    rw [hx₂_zero] at hx₂
748	    simpa using hx₂.symm
749	  · -- (3) min(depth N, depth N'' + 1) ≤ depth N'
750	    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
751	    intro a ha
752	    rw [le_min_iff] at ha
753	    obtain ⟨haN, haN''add⟩ := ha
754	    rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
755	    intro i hi e
756	    -- `e : Ext κ S.X₁ i = Ext κ (of R N') i`; goal `e = 0`.
757	    have hicast : (i : ℕ∞) < (a : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast hi
758	    have hiN : (i : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N := hicast.trans_le haN
759	    -- `e ∘ S.f ∈ Ext κ (of R N) i = 0`.
760	    have hef : e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero i) = 0 :=
761	      ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hiN _
762	    -- Split on `i = 0` vs `i ≥ 1`. For `i ≥ 1`, use `covariant_sequence_exact₁`.
763	    -- For `i = 0`, postcomposition by `S.f` is injective (since `S.f` is mono).
764	    rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos i with hi0 | hi0
765	    · subst hi0
766	      -- `e : Ext κ S.X₁ 0`; postcomp by `S.f` is injective; image is `e ∘ S.f = 0`,
767	      -- so `e = 0`.
768	      have hmono : CategoryTheory.Mono S.f :=
769	        (ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective _).mpr _hf
770	      have hinj := CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono κ S.f
771	      apply hinj
772	      simpa using hef
773	    · -- `i ≥ 1`. Let `i = j + 1` and use `covariant_sequence_exact₁` at
774	      -- `n₀ = j, n₁ = i = j + 1`.
775	      obtain ⟨j, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hi0)
776	      -- `e : Ext κ (of R N') (j + 1)`. We need `Ext κ (of R N'') j = 0`.
777	      -- From `↑(j+2) ≤ ↑a ≤ depth N'' + 1`, get `↑j + 1 ≤ depth N''`, so `↑j < depth N''`.
778	      have hjN'' : (j : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' := by
779	        have hja : j + 2 ≤ a := by omega
780	        have h_j2 : ((j + 2 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' + 1 := by
781	          refine le_trans ?_ haN''add
782	          exact_mod_cast hja
783	        have hcast : ((j + 2 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = ((j + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) + 1 := by push_cast; ring
784	        rw [hcast] at h_j2
785	        have h_canc : ((j + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' :=
786	          (ENat.add_le_add_iff_right (by norm_num : (1 : ℕ∞) ≠ ⊤)).mp h_j2
787	        have hcast2 : ((j + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = (j : ℕ∞) + 1 := by push_cast; ring
788	        rw [hcast2] at h_canc
789	        exact (ENat.add_one_le_iff (by simp : (j : ℕ∞) ≠ ⊤)).mp h_canc
790	      obtain ⟨x₃, hx₃⟩ :=
791	        CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁ κ hS e hef rfl
792	      -- `x₃ ∈ Ext κ (of R N'') j = 0`.
793	      have hx₃_zero : x₃ = 0 := ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hjN'' _
794	      rw [hx₃_zero] at hx₃
795	      simpa using hx₃.symm
796	
797	/-! ### Helper iter-193 Lane G (axiom-clean): depth is preserved under R-linear equivalence
798	
799	For a commutative ring `R`, an ideal `I ⊆ R`, and two `R`-modules `M, M'` with
800	an `R`-linear equivalence `e : M ≃ₗ[R] M'`, we have `depth I M = depth I M'`.
801	
802	This is the standard "depth is an invariant of the isomorphism class" fact;
803	the proof has two steps: (1) the side-condition `I • ⊤ = ⊤` is preserved
804	under linear equivalence, and (2) the regular-sequence supremum sets agree
805	via `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr`. Both halves are direct Mathlib calls; the
806	helper is axiom-clean.
807	
808	iter-193 Lane G structural advance: this helper is the substrate piece
809	needed to identify `depth(M)` with `depth(R^k)` for `M` finite free, which
810	in turn closes the `pd(M) = 0` base case of the Auslander–Buchsbaum
811	formula modulo the `depth(R^k) = depth(R)` direct-sum step (a separate
812	substrate item; the file's residual sorries are narrowed to that one
813	named piece). -/
814	lemma depth_eq_of_linearEquiv {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (I : Ideal R)
815	    {M M' : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [AddCommGroup M'] [Module R M']
816	    (e : M ≃ₗ[R] M') :
817	    depth I M = depth I M' := by
818	  -- Step 1: `I • ⊤ = ⊤` is preserved under the linear equivalence.
819	  have hcond : (I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) = ⊤) ↔ (I • (⊤ : Submodule R M') = ⊤) := by
820	    have e_top : Submodule.map (e : M →ₗ[R] M') (⊤ : Submodule R M) = ⊤ := by
821	      rw [Submodule.map_top]; exact LinearEquiv.range e
822	    have e_symm_top :
823	        Submodule.map (e.symm : M' →ₗ[R] M) (⊤ : Submodule R M') = ⊤ := by
824	      rw [Submodule.map_top]; exact LinearEquiv.range e.symm
825	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
826	    · intro h
827	      have hmap :=
828	        Submodule.map_smul'' I (⊤ : Submodule R M) (e : M →ₗ[R] M')
829	      rw [h, e_top] at hmap
830	      exact hmap.symm
831	    · intro h
832	      have hmap :=
833	        Submodule.map_smul'' I (⊤ : Submodule R M') (e.symm : M' →ₗ[R] M)
834	      rw [h, e_symm_top] at hmap
835	      exact hmap.symm
836	  -- Step 2: the `sSup` sets agree via `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr`.
837	  unfold depth
838	  by_cases h : I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) = ⊤
839	  · simp [if_pos h, if_pos (hcond.mp h)]
840	  · rw [if_neg h, if_neg (mt hcond.mpr h)]
841	    congr 1
842	    ext n
843	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
844	    · rintro ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, hreg⟩
845	      exact ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, (LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr e rs).mp hreg⟩
846	    · rintro ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, hreg⟩
847	      exact ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, (LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr e rs).mpr hreg⟩
848	
849	/-! ### Helper iter-194 Lane G (axiom-clean): depth of constant Pi equals depth of fiber.
850	
851	For a commutative ring `R`, ideal `I`, module `M`, and nonempty finite type `ι`,
852	`depth I (ι → M) = depth I M`. The proof goes through the regular-sequence
853	characterization: each `r`-action on `ι → M` is pointwise (so an `r ∈ R` is
854	regular on `ι → M` iff regular on `M`), and the quotient `(ι → M)/r·⊤`
855	identifies with `ι → M/r·⊤` via `Submodule.quotientPi`. The side condition
856	`I • ⊤ = ⊤` agrees on both sides via a `Pi.single` lifting argument.
857	
858	This is the substrate needed to close the `pd_R(M) = 0` case of the
859	Auslander–Buchsbaum formula (where `M ≃ₗ[R] Fin k → R` via a basis). -/
860	
861	/-- For any commutative ring `R`, ideal `I`, finite index `ι`, and module `M`,
862	the ideal-action `I • ⊤_{ι → M}` equals the pi-submodule of fibre `I • ⊤_M`s. -/
863	private lemma ideal_smul_top_pi_const
864	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι]
865	    (I : Ideal R) {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
866	    (I • (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M))) =
867	      Submodule.pi (Set.univ : Set ι) (fun (_ : ι) => I • (⊤ : Submodule R M)) := by
868	  apply le_antisymm
869	  · intro f hf i _
870	    refine Submodule.smul_induction_on hf ?_ ?_
871	    · intro a hain x _
872	      change a • x i ∈ I • (⊤ : Submodule R M)
873	      exact Submodule.smul_mem_smul hain trivial
874	    · intro x y hx hy
875	      change (x + y) i ∈ _
876	      exact Submodule.add_mem _ hx hy
877	  · intro f hf
878	    rw [show f = ∑ j, Pi.single j (f j) from (Finset.univ_sum_single f).symm]
879	    refine Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_
880	    intro j _
881	    have hfj : f j ∈ I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) := hf j (Set.mem_univ j)
882	    have hmap :
883	        Pi.single j (f j) ∈
884	          Submodule.map (LinearMap.single R (fun (_ : ι) => M) j)
885	            (I • (⊤ : Submodule R M)) :=
886	      Submodule.mem_map.mpr ⟨f j, hfj, rfl⟩
887	    rw [Submodule.map_smul''] at hmap
888	    exact Submodule.smul_mono le_rfl le_top hmap
889	
890	/-- The side condition `I • ⊤ = ⊤` agrees on `ι → M` and `M` for nonempty
891	finite `ι`: a free product of fibre `I•⊤_M`-witnesses combines to a
892	`I•⊤_{ι → M}`-witness (via `Pi.single`-lifting), and conversely a
893	`Pi.single j m`-projection at `j` reads off the witness on the fibre. -/
894	private lemma ideal_smul_top_pi_const_eq_top_iff
895	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] [Nonempty ι]
896	    (I : Ideal R) {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
897	    I • (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M)) = ⊤ ↔ I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) = ⊤ := by
898	  constructor
899	  · intro h
900	    rw [eq_top_iff]
901	    intro m _
902	    obtain ⟨j⟩ := ‹Nonempty ι›
903	    have hsingle_mem :
904	        (Pi.single j m : ι → M) ∈ I • (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M)) := by
905	      rw [h]; trivial
906	    rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const] at hsingle_mem
907	    have := hsingle_mem j (Set.mem_univ j)
908	    rwa [Pi.single_eq_same] at this
909	  · intro h
910	    rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const, eq_top_iff]
911	    intro f _ i _
912	    rw [h]
913	    trivial
914	
915	/-- `QuotSMulTop r (ι → M) ≃ₗ[R] ι → QuotSMulTop r M` for finite `ι`,
916	obtained by rewriting `r • ⊤ = Ideal.span {r} • ⊤` and using
917	`Submodule.quotientPi`. -/
918	private noncomputable def quotSMulTopPiConstLinearEquiv
919	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] (r : R)
920	    {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
921	    QuotSMulTop r (ι → M) ≃ₗ[R] (ι → QuotSMulTop r M) := by
922	  refine (Submodule.quotEquivOfEq _ _ ?_).trans (Submodule.quotientPi _)
923	  rw [← Submodule.ideal_span_singleton_smul r (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M))]
924	  rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const]
925	  congr 1
926	  funext _
927	  exact Submodule.ideal_span_singleton_smul r ⊤
928	
929	/-- For nonempty finite `ι`, a list `rs : List R` is `(ι → M)`-regular iff it
930	is `M`-regular. Proof by induction on `rs`: the empty case reduces to
931	`Nontrivial (ι → M) ↔ Nontrivial M`; the cons case uses `Pi.isSMulRegular_iff`
932	(for the SMul-regular conjunct) plus `quotSMulTopPiConstLinearEquiv` +
933	`LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr` (to bridge the quotient regularity to the IH on
934	`QuotSMulTop r M`). -/
935	private lemma isRegular_pi_const_iff_of_nonempty
936	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] [Nonempty ι]
937	    (rs : List R) :
938	    ∀ {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M],
939	      RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular (ι → M) rs ↔
940	        RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs := by
941	  induction rs with
942	  | nil =>
943	    intro M _ _
944	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
945	    · rintro ⟨_, hPi_top⟩
946	      refine ⟨.nil R M, ?_⟩
947	      rw [Ideal.ofList_nil, Submodule.bot_smul] at hPi_top ⊢
948	      intro habs
949	      apply hPi_top
950	      rw [Submodule.eq_bot_iff] at habs ⊢
951	      intro f _
952	      funext i
953	      exact habs (f i) trivial
954	    · rintro ⟨_, hM_top⟩
955	      refine ⟨.nil R (ι → M), ?_⟩
956	      rw [Ideal.ofList_nil, Submodule.bot_smul] at hM_top ⊢
957	      obtain ⟨j⟩ := ‹Nonempty ι›
958	      intro habs
959	      apply hM_top
960	      rw [Submodule.eq_bot_iff] at habs ⊢
961	      intro m _
962	      have hsingle : (Pi.single j m : ι → M) = 0 := habs _ trivial
963	      have heval := congr_fun hsingle j
964	      rwa [Pi.single_eq_same, Pi.zero_apply] at heval
965	  | cons r rs' ih =>
966	    intro M _ _
967	    rw [RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff, RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff]
968	    refine and_congr ?_ ?_
969	    · constructor
970	      · intro h
971	        obtain ⟨j⟩ := ‹Nonempty ι›
972	        exact Pi.isSMulRegular_iff.mp h j
973	      · intro h
974	        exact Pi.isSMulRegular_iff.mpr fun _ => h
975	    · rw [LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr
976	        (quotSMulTopPiConstLinearEquiv (R := R) (ι := ι) r (M := M)) rs']
977	      exact ih (M := QuotSMulTop r M)
978	
979	/-- **Main iter-194 Lane G closure.** For any commutative ring `R`, ideal `I`,
980	`R`-module `M`, and nonempty finite type `ι`, the depth of the Pi module
981	`ι → M` equals the depth of `M`:
982	```
983	  depth I (ι → M) = depth I M.
984	```
985	This is the substrate for the `pd_R(M) = 0` case of the Auslander–Buchsbaum
986	formula: a finite free module `M ≃ₗ[R] Fin k → R` has `depth(M) = depth(R)`,
987	so `0 + depth(M) = depth(R)` holds. -/
988	lemma depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty
989	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (I : Ideal R)
990	    {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] [Nonempty ι]
991	    {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
992	    depth I (ι → M) = depth I M := by
993	  unfold depth
994	  by_cases h : I • (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M)) = ⊤
995	  · rw [if_pos h, if_pos ((ideal_smul_top_pi_const_eq_top_iff I).mp h)]
996	  · rw [if_neg h, if_neg (mt (ideal_smul_top_pi_const_eq_top_iff I).mpr h)]
997	    congr 1
998	    ext n
999	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
1000	    · rintro ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, hreg⟩
1001	      exact ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, (isRegular_pi_const_iff_of_nonempty rs).mp hreg⟩
1002	    · rintro ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, hreg⟩
1003	      exact ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, (isRegular_pi_const_iff_of_nonempty rs).mpr hreg⟩
1004	
1005	end Module

1007	/-! ## §5. The Auslander–Buchsbaum formula
1008	
1009	For a nonzero finite module `M` of finite projective dimension over a
1010	Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, the **Auslander–Buchsbaum formula** reads
1011	```
1012	  pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)
1013	```
1014	(Stacks tag 090V). The proof inducts on `depth(M)`: the base case
1015	`depth(M) = 0` uses a minimal finite free resolution of `M` and the
1016	"what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) plus iterated application of the
1017	depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma (§4) to bound `depth(R)` against the
1018	resolution length; the inductive step picks a common non-zero-divisor
1019	`x ∈ 𝔪` on both `R` and `M`, applies the snake lemma to obtain a minimal
1020	finite free resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the same length, and uses
1021	the inductive hypothesis on `M/xM` over `R/xR`.
1022	
1023	Blueprint reference: `thm:auslander_buchsbaum` (Stacks tag 090V). -/
1024	
1025	/-! ### Lane G iter-195 structural carving: inductive-step helper.
1026	
1027	The `pd_R(M) = k + 1` inductive step of the Auslander–Buchsbaum formula is
1028	extracted into the helper `auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd` below. This
1029	isolates the entire substrate-gap content of the inductive step into a
1030	single named typed-`sorry` declaration with a precise iter-196+
1031	re-engagement plan, rather than an opaque inline `sorry` at the case-split
1032	site. The main theorem then dispatches the `n > 0` branch by a one-line
1033	`exact` call to the helper.
1034	
1035	Per the iter-195 Lane G directive (OFF-CRITICAL-PATH minimal dispatch),
1036	this is a **structural commit** (option (b): carving with iter-196 re-
1037	engagement timeline). The HARD BAR is met; closure is deferred. -/
1038	
1039	/-- **Lane G iter-195 structural carving (typed-`sorry` substrate-gap helper).**
1040	
1041	The `pd_R(M) = k + 1` inductive step of the Auslander–Buchsbaum formula.
1042	
1043	**Blueprint proof shape** (`sec:ab_main` inductive step, Stacks 090V).
1044	Strong induction on `depth(M)`:
1045	
1046	* **Sub-case `depth(M) = 0`.** Let `e := pd_R(M) = k + 1`. By Stacks
1047	  `lemma-add-trivial-complex` (minimal-finite-free-resolution carving),
1048	  `M` admits a minimal resolution
1049	  `0 → R^{n_e} → R^{n_{e-1}} → ⋯ → R^{n_0} → M → 0` with all matrix
1050	  coefficients in `𝔪`. The "what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF)
1051	  gives `depth(R) ≥ e`. Iterating `depth_of_short_exact` on the
1052	  short-exact splits `0 → R^{n_e} → R^{n_{e-1}} → K_{e-2} → 0`, …,
1053	  `0 → K_0 → R^{n_0} → M → 0` gives
1054	  `depth(K_{e-c}) ≥ depth(R) - (c - 1)` and finally
1055	  `depth(M) ≥ depth(R) - e`. Since `depth(M) = 0`, also `depth(R) ≤ e`,
1056	  hence `depth(R) = e = (k + 1) + depth(M)`.
1057	
1058	* **Sub-case `depth(M) > 0`.** Pick `x ∈ 𝔪` a common NZD on both `R`
1059	  and `M` (Stacks `lemma-depth-sum-direct-sum` ⟹ `depth(R ⊕ M) > 0`,
1060	  hence a common NZD via prime avoidance over `Ass(R) ∪ Ass(M)`). The
1061	  snake lemma applied to multiplication by `x` on a minimal resolution
1062	  of `M` produces a minimal resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the
1063	  same length, so `pd_{R/xR}(M/xM) = k + 1`. By Stacks
1064	  `lemma-depth-drops-by-one`, `depth(R/xR) = depth(R) - 1` and
1065	  `depth(M/xM) = depth(M) - 1`, with regular sequences in `𝔪`
1066	  corresponding to those in `𝔪/(x)`, so
1067	  `depth_R(M/xM) = depth_{R/xR}(M/xM)`. The IH on `M/xM` over `R/xR`
1068	  gives `(k + 1) + (depth(M) - 1) = depth(R) - 1`, i.e.
1069	  `(k + 1) + depth(M) = depth(R)`.

1304	/-! ### Helper 2.0 (iter-187 Lane G sub-lane G1): cotangent dim-drop on `R ⧸ (x)`
1305	
1306	**Statement.** For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` and `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²`, the
1307	cotangent space of `R / (x)` has dimension one less than that of `R`:
1308	```
1309	finrank κ' (CotangentSpace (R/(x))) + 1 = finrank κ (CotangentSpace R)
1310	```
1311	where `κ = R / 𝔪` and `κ' = (R/(x)) / 𝔪'` are the two residue fields
1312	(canonically isomorphic via the natural quotient `R/𝔪 ≃ (R/(x))/𝔪'`).
1313	
1314	**Role.** This is the cotangent-space dim-drop building block for
1315	`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ` below: once Stacks 00NQ
1316	(regular local ⟹ domain) is filled in, this dim-drop is what upgrades
1317	`R/(x)` of dimension `k` back to `IsRegularLocalRing` via
1318	`IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`.
1319	
1320	**Proof structure** (Bourbaki-style via `Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank`):
1321	1. The R-algebra map `π : R → R/(x)` induces a κ-linear surjection of
1322	   cotangent spaces `f : 𝔪.Cotangent → 𝔪'.Cotangent` via
1323	   `Ideal.mapCotangent_surjective_of_comap_eq` (which applies because
1324	   `(maximalIdeal R).comap π = ker π ⊔ maximalIdeal R = (x) ⊔ 𝔪 = 𝔪`).
1325	2. The kernel of `f` is the R-submodule generated by the image `x̄` of `x`
1326	   in `𝔪.Cotangent` (via `Ideal.mapCotangent_ker_of_surjective` — the
1327	   kernel is `(Submodule.comap 𝔪.subtype ((x) ⊓ 𝔪)).map 𝔪.toCotangent`
1328	   which collapses to `Submodule.span R {x̄}` because `(x) ⊆ 𝔪`).
1329	3. The R-action on 𝔪.Cotangent factors through κ (it's a κ-vector space),
1330	   so this is also `Submodule.span κ {x̄}`.
1331	4. `x ∉ 𝔪²` ⟺ `x̄ ≠ 0` (Helper 1.5), giving
1332	   `finrank κ (Submodule.span κ {x̄}) = 1` via `finrank_span_singleton`.
1333	5. Combining via `Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank` and the
1334	   `LinearMap.quotKerEquivOfSurjective` isomorphism, we get the formula.
1335	   The residue-field switch `κ → κ'` is handled by
1336	   `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`, which
1337	   reduces the κ-finrank to spanFinrank (a ring-theoretic invariant) and
1338	   the spanFinrank-equality is purely numeric.

1341	as a single named typed sorry (the κ-subspace identification step). The

1348	* `finrank_span_singleton` (Mathlib b80f227 ✓)
1349	* `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`

1598	/-! ### Helper 2 (substantive typed `sorry`): system-of-parameters as a regular sequence
1599	
1600	For a regular local ring `R` of Krull dimension `d = (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank`,
1601	a minimal generating set `x_1, …, x_d` of the maximal ideal `𝔪` is an `R`-regular
1602	sequence. This is the **lower bound** half of Stacks 00OD.
1603	
1604	The proof in Stacks 00OD uses:
1605	1. A regular local ring is an integral domain (Stacks 00NQ).
1606	2. For each `c < d`, the quotient `R / (x_1, …, x_c)` is again a regular local
1607	   ring, of Krull dimension `d - c` (Krull's principal ideal theorem +
1608	   `lemma-one-equation`).
1609	
1610	Step (1) — `IsRegularLocalRing R ⟹ IsDomain R` — is **not present in Mathlib at
1611	the pinned commit** (`b80f227`); a `lean_leansearch` for "regular local ring is
1612	a domain" returns nothing relevant. Step (2) requires the regular-quotient
1613	inductive structure (Stacks 00NQ + height-one quotient regularity), which is
1614	similarly absent. Both are substantive multi-iter content.
1615	
1616	The signature here is the non-tautological existence statement: a list `rs : List R`
1617	with `rs.length = (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank`, `rs ⊆ maximalIdeal R`, and
1618	`IsRegular R rs`. Downstream, `of_regular` consumes this directly to close the
1619	`depth ≥ d` lower bound.
1620	
1621	iter-185 Lane G **STRUCTURAL SCAFFOLD**: the main lemma is structurally
1622	complete by strong induction on `n = spanFinrank R`. The inductive step is
1623	factored into `regularLocal_inductive_step` (axiom-clean: lift-and-cons
1624	assembly via Mathlib's `IsRegular.cons'`) plus the Mathlib-gap helper
1625	`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ` (typed `sorry`,
1626	Stacks 00NQ + 00NU consolidated). The substrate work (`IsRegularLocalRing ⟹
1627	IsDomain` + Krull's PIT for quotient-by-minimal-generator) is genuinely
1628	multi-iter; the iter-185 progress is the structural extraction of the
1629	Mathlib gap into a narrow, well-typed helper, leaving the assembly
1630	axiom-clean for the gap-fill iteration to finish in `~5 LOC` once the
1631	helper closes. -/
1632	
1633	/-! ### Narrow substrate gap (Stacks 00NQ): regular local ring is a domain
1634	
1635	The Auslander–Buchsbaum-route closure of "regular local ring is Cohen–Macaulay"
1636	ultimately reduces to **Stacks 00NQ**: every regular local Noetherian ring
1637	is an integral domain. Mathlib at the pinned commit `b80f227` does not expose
1638	this implication (`lean_leansearch` for "regular local ring is a domain"
1639	returns nothing relevant; `lean_loogle` for `IsRegularLocalRing _ → IsDomain _`
1640	returns empty).
1641	
1642	Stacks 00NQ proof sketch (induction on `dim R = spanFinrank 𝔪`):
1643	* Base `dim R = 0`: then `𝔪 = ⊥`, hence `R` is a field, hence a domain.
1644	* Step `dim R = d + 1 ≥ 1`:
1645	  - Pick `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²` (axiom-clean via Nakayama; see
1646	    `exists_notMemSq_of_spanFinrank_pos` below).
1647	  - By the cotangent dim-drop (`finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ`,
1648	    iter-188 Lane G1 closure) **plus** Krull's principal ideal theorem
1649	    (`ringKrullDim_quotient_span_singleton_succ_eq_ringKrullDim_of_mem_jacobson`
1650	    gives the `≥` half), `R / (x)` is regular local of dim `d`.
1651	  - By induction, `R / (x)` is a domain, hence `(x)` is a prime ideal of `R`.
1652	  - Let `𝔭` be a minimal prime of `R` with `𝔭 ⊆ (x)`. For `y ∈ 𝔭`, write
1653	    `y = xz`; since `𝔭` is prime and `x ∉ 𝔭` (else `x ∈ 𝔭 ⊆ (x)` doesn't
1654	    immediately give a contradiction but combined with `dim R/(x) = d` and
1655	    `x ∈ 𝔭` height-zero we get `𝔭 = 0` which is the conclusion), we get
1656	    `z ∈ 𝔭`. Hence `𝔭 ⊆ x 𝔭`, so iterating, `𝔭 ⊆ ∩ x^n 𝔭 ⊆ ∩ 𝔪^n = 0`
1657	    (Krull intersection). So `𝔭 = 0`, and `R` is a domain.
1658	
1659	Full formalization is ~300 LOC (involves Krull's PIT, prime avoidance,
1660	Krull intersection, minimal-primes-are-finite); deferred to a future iter
1661	or Mathlib upstream contribution. -/
1662	
1663	/-- **Axiom-clean Nakayama witness.** For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`
1664	with `spanFinrank 𝔪 ≥ 1`, there exists `x ∈ 𝔪` with `x ∉ 𝔪²`.
1665	
1666	This is the "cotangent space is nonzero" content: by Nakayama, if `𝔪 ⊆ 𝔪²`
1667	then `𝔪 = 0` (so `spanFinrank 𝔪 = 0`), contradicting the hypothesis. -/
1668	private lemma exists_notMemSq_of_spanFinrank_pos
1669	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1670	    (h : 0 < (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank) :
1671	    ∃ x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R, x ∉ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ^ 2 := by
1672	  -- By contradiction: assume 𝔪 ⊆ 𝔪², then by Nakayama 𝔪 = 0, but spanFinrank 𝔪 ≥ 1.
1673	  by_contra h_neg
1674	  push Not at h_neg
1675	  have h𝔪_le_sq : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R ≤ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ^ 2 := h_neg
1676	  -- 𝔪² = 𝔪 • 𝔪.
1677	  have hsq : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) ^ 2
1678	      = (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) := by
1679	    rw [sq, ← Ideal.smul_eq_mul]
1680	  have h𝔪_fg : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R).FG :=
1681	    Ideal.fg_of_isNoetherianRing _
1682	  -- Use Submodule.le_of_le_smul_of_le_jacobson_bot with N = ⊥.
1683	  have hjac : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ≤ (⊥ : Ideal R).jacobson :=
1684	    IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _
1685	  have h_le_smul : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R)
1686	      ≤ ⊥ ⊔ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) •
1687	        (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) := by
1688	    rw [bot_sup_eq, ← hsq]; exact h𝔪_le_sq
1689	  have h𝔪_bot : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) ≤ ⊥ :=
1690	    Submodule.le_of_le_smul_of_le_jacobson_bot h𝔪_fg hjac h_le_smul
1691	  have h𝔪_eq_bot : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) = ⊥ := le_bot_iff.mp h𝔪_bot
1692	  have h_span : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = 0 := by
1693	    rw [h𝔪_eq_bot]; exact Submodule.spanFinrank_bot
1694	  omega

1696	/-! ### Helper for Stacks 00NQ base case (iter-190 Lane G, axiom-clean).
1697	
1698	For a Noetherian local ring `R` with `(maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = 0`, the
1699	ring `R` is a field, hence a domain. The maximal ideal collapses to `⊥` via
1700	`Submodule.spanFinrank_eq_zero_iff_eq_bot` (under FG), and
1701	`IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq` upgrades the resulting field-by-
1702	trivial-maximal-ideal characterisation to `IsField R`, from which
1703	`IsField.isDomain` gives `IsDomain R`. -/
1704	private lemma isDomain_of_isLocalRing_of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_zero
1705	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1706	    (h : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = 0) : IsDomain R := by
