351	/-! ### Flat base change of cohomology (Stacks tag 02KH, `i = 0` form)
352	
353	Let
354	```
355	   g'
356	X' ───→ X
357	│       │
358	f'      f
359	↓       ↓
360	S' ───→ S
361	   g
362	```
363	be a cartesian square of schemes with `g` flat and `f` quasi-compact
364	quasi-separated. Let `F` be a sheaf of `O_X`-modules. Then the canonical
365	base-change map `g* (f_* F) ⟶ f'_* ((g')* F)` is an isomorphism in
366	`S'.Modules`.
367	
368	(The full Stacks 02KH statement covers all higher direct images
369	`R^i f_* F` for `i ≥ 0`; the `i = 0` form encoded here is the
370	substantive content of `lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology(ii)` of
371	Stacks 02KH, with the `i ≥ 1` form post-iter-177 work after the
372	higher-direct-image bifunctor lands.)
373	
374	iter-177 (Lane QS-FLAT): the body constructs the canonical base-change
375	natural transformation via the mate equivalence of the
376	`pullback ⊣ pushforward` adjunction (Mathlib's `mateEquiv` of
377	`Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction`), then exhibits the iso
378	via the `canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso` helper. The deep mathematical
379	content (Stacks tag 02KH / 02KE / 00H8) lives entirely in the helper;
380	it reduces affine-locally to: for a flat ring map `A → B` and an
381	`A`-algebra `R`, the canonical map `B ⊗_A H^i(X, F) → H^i(X_B, F_B)`
382	is an iso for any quasi-coherent `F` (the `i = 0` form is what we use).
383	The helper remains a typed `sorry` pending the affine-local reduction
384	+ algebraic flat base change; this is iter-178+ body work after
385	quasi-compact open-cover Mayer-Vietoris infrastructure is in scope. -/
386	
387	/-- The canonical base-change natural transformation `g* (f_* -) ⟶ f'_* ((g')* -)`
388	associated to a cartesian square
389	```
390	     g'
391	X' ─────→ X
392	│         │
393	f'        f
394	↓         ↓
395	S' ─────→ S
396	     g
397	```
398	in `Scheme`. Constructed as the *mate* (Beck–Chevalley transform)
399	under the `pullback ⊣ pushforward` adjunctions on sheaves of modules
400	of the canonical 2-isomorphism
401	`pullback g ⋙ pullback f' ≅ pullback f ⋙ pullback g'` coming from
402	`g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`.
403	
404	This natural transformation always exists (no flatness needed). The
405	content of the flat base-change theorem (Stacks tag 02KH) is the
406	*isomorphism* claim under the hypotheses
407	`[QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] [Flat g]`; that claim is the
408	helper `canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso`. -/
409	noncomputable def canonicalBaseChangeMap
410	    {X X' S S' : Scheme.{u}}
411	    {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
412	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g) :
413	    Scheme.Modules.pushforward f ⋙ Scheme.Modules.pullback g ⟶
414	      Scheme.Modules.pullback g' ⋙ Scheme.Modules.pushforward f' :=
415	  CategoryTheory.mateEquiv
416	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)
417	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f')
418	      (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f' g) ≪≫
419	        Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr sq.w.symm ≪≫
420	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g' f).symm).hom)
421	
422	/-- **Trivial bridge** (pushforward of pullback at sections — rfl).
423	
424	The section of `(pushforward f').obj ((pullback g').obj F)` over an
425	open `U ⊆ S'` identifies definitionally with the section of
426	`(pullback g').obj F` over `f' ⁻¹ᵁ U`, by `Scheme.Modules.pushforward_obj_obj`.
427	Factored as a separate (closed) lemma to document step (3) of the
428	intended-body plan in
429	`canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_isAffineOpen` cleanly. -/
430	private lemma pushforward_pullback_section_eq_pullback_section
431	    {X X' S' : Scheme.{u}} (f' : X' ⟶ S') (g' : X' ⟶ X)
432	    (F : X.Modules) (U : S'.Opens) :
433	    Γ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f').obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F), U) =
434	      Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F, f' ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

686	This pin captures the Step 3 transport content of the Tilde-isoTop route:
687	the top section of a sheaf pulled back along
688	`IsAffineOpen.fromSpec : Spec Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Y` is canonically `Γ(Y, U)`-linearly
689	identified with the section over `U` itself. Substantive content combines
690	`AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.isoTop` (Mathlib HAS) with the `hU.isoSpec`
691	transport (Mathlib gap at `b80f227`).

693	iter-190 closure (Lane F Step 3 HARD BAR): the body chains
694	`Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback` (Mathlib's identification of the
695	`pullback` functor with the `restrict` functor along an open immersion;
696	applicable since `hU.fromSpec` carries `IsOpenImmersion` via
697	`IsAffineOpen.isOpenImmersion_fromSpec`) with the definitional
698	`Scheme.Modules.restrict_obj` (sections of `N.restrict f` over `V` equal
699	sections of `N` over `f ''ᵁ V`, by `rfl`) and the propositional
700	`Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange` + `IsAffineOpen.opensRange_fromSpec`
701	to identify `hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤ = U`. -/
702	private theorem pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict
703	    {Y : Scheme.{u}} (N : Y.Modules) {U : Y.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) :
704	    -- `Γ(Y, U)`-linear identification between the top section of the pullback
705	    -- (along `hU.fromSpec : Spec Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Y`) and `Γ(N, U)` itself. The
706	    -- module-action ring on the LHS is set up via the canonical algebra
707	    -- `Γ(Y, U) → Γ((Spec Γ(Y, U)), ⊤)`, which is the structure-sheaf
708	    -- equivalence on the affine scheme.
709	    letI : Algebra Γ(Y, U) Γ((Spec Γ(Y, U)), ⊤) :=
710	      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom.toAlgebra
711	    letI : Module Γ(Y, U) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) :=
712	      Module.compHom _ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom
713	    Nonempty (Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) ≃ₗ[Γ(Y, U)]
714	      Γ(N, U)) := by
715	  letI algInst : Algebra Γ(Y, U) Γ((Spec Γ(Y, U)), ⊤) :=
716	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom.toAlgebra
717	  letI modInst : Module Γ(Y, U) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) :=
718	    Module.compHom _ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom
719	  -- Step 1: Identify pullback along `hU.fromSpec` with the restriction functor.
720	  -- Mathlib's `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` gives this for any open immersion;
721	  -- `hU.fromSpec` is an open immersion by `IsAffineOpen.isOpenImmersion_fromSpec`.
722	  have isoSheaf : (Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N ≅ N.restrict hU.fromSpec :=
723	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback hU.fromSpec).app N).symm
724	  -- Step 2: The image of ⊤ under hU.fromSpec equals U (Stacks 01HH-style bridge).
725	  have hImg : (hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ (⊤ : (Spec Γ(Y, U)).Opens) : Y.Opens) = U := by
726	    rw [Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange]; exact hU.opensRange_fromSpec
727	  -- Step 3: section-level map from the iso, then the rfl identification
728	  -- `Γ(N.restrict hU.fromSpec, ⊤) = Γ(N, hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤)` (per
729	  -- `Scheme.Modules.restrict_obj`), then a presheaf restriction along the
730	  -- propositional equality `hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤ = U` to land in `Γ(N, U)`.
731	  -- Define the additive equivalence.
732	  let toFun : Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) → Γ(N, U) := fun x =>
733	    (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op).hom ((Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤).hom x)
734	  let invFun : Γ(N, U) → Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) := fun y =>
735	    (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.inv ⊤).hom ((N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg).op).hom y)
736	  have left_inv : Function.LeftInverse invFun toFun := by
737	    intro x
738	    simp only [invFun, toFun, ← AddCommGrpCat.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp,
739	      eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_refl, op_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id,
740	      AddCommGrpCat.hom_id, AddMonoidHom.id_apply,
741	      ← Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app, isoSheaf.hom_inv_id, Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app]
742	  have right_inv : Function.RightInverse invFun toFun := by
743	    intro y
744	    simp only [invFun, toFun, ← AddCommGrpCat.comp_apply, ← Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app,
745	      isoSheaf.inv_hom_id, Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app,
746	      AddCommGrpCat.hom_id, AddMonoidHom.id_apply,
747	      ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_refl, op_id,
748	      CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
749	  have map_add' : ∀ x y, toFun (x + y) = toFun x + toFun y := by
750	    intro x y
751	    change (AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op))
752	      ((AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤)) (x + y)) =
753	      _ + _
754	    rw [show ((AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤)) (x + y)) =
755	      (AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤)) x +
756	      (AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤)) y from
757	      AddMonoidHom.map_add _ _ _]
758	    exact AddMonoidHom.map_add _ _ _
759	  let addEq : Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) ≃+ Γ(N, U) :=
760	    { toFun := toFun
761	      invFun := invFun
762	      left_inv := left_inv
763	      right_inv := right_inv
764	      map_add' := map_add' }
765	  -- Upgrade to a `Γ(Y, U)`-LinearEquiv via the smul compatibility.
766	  refine ⟨addEq.toLinearEquiv ?_⟩
767	  -- Smul-compatibility:
768	  intro r x
769	  -- The LHS `r • x` is `Module.compHom`-action: `r • x = (ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom r • x`
770	  -- with the natural Γ(Spec Γ(Y, U), ⊤)-action on the pullback module sheaf at ⊤.
771	  -- Step A: Reduce r • x on the LHS to (ΓSpecIso).inv.hom r • x with natural action.
772	  change (AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op))
773	    ((AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤))
774	      ((CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv) r • x)) = _
775	  -- Step B: Apply Hom.app_smul (Γ(Spec ⊤)-linearity of the SheafOfModules iso),
776	  -- which migrates the scalar through `Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤`.
777	  rw [Scheme.Modules.Hom.app_smul]
778	  -- Step C (iter-192 Lane F closure): the residual identity is the substantive
779	  -- Stacks 01HH-style ring compatibility:
780	  --   Y.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op
781	  --     ((hU.fromSpec.appIso ⊤).inv ((ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom r)) = r,
782	  -- combined with `Scheme.Modules.map_smul` to pull the algebra-map image
783	  -- through the presheaf restriction.
784	  --
785	  -- The recipe (per `analogies/lane-f-restrictscalars-smul.md`):
786	  -- Step A: aliasing-`let` `y : Γ(N, hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤)` to make the smul-unfold
787	  -- on the restrict-of-N section visible as a Y-side action via
788	  -- `restrictFunctor`'s definition (smul is `(appIso ⊤).inv.hom s` on Y-side).
789	  -- Step B: `Scheme.Modules.map_smul` to migrate the scalar through the
790	  -- presheaf restriction.
791	  -- Step C: the categorical key identity
792	  --   (ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫ (hU.fromSpec.appIso ⊤).inv ≫
793	  --     Y.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op = 𝟙 _
794	  -- via `appLE_appIso_inv` + `fromSpec_app_self` + `Hom.appLE` unfolding.
795	  -- Step A: aliasing-`let` to bring the Y-side smul into instance scope.
796	  set y : ↑Γ(N, hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤) := (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤).hom x
797	    with hy
798	  -- Step A continued: the Γ(Spec _, ⊤)-smul on `y` (under its restrict-of-N
799	  -- view) is rfl-equal to the Y-side smul via `(hU.fromSpec.appIso ⊤).inv`.
800	  change (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op).hom
801	    (((hU.fromSpec.appIso ⊤).inv.hom ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso Γ(Y, U)).inv.hom r)) • y) =
802	    r • (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op).hom y
803	  -- Step B: migrate the Y-side scalar through `N.presheaf.map`.
804	  rw [Scheme.Modules.map_smul]
805	  -- Step C: reduce the scalar identity to the categorical key identity.
806	  congr 1
807	  -- Goal: (Y.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op).hom
808	  --        ((hU.fromSpec.appIso ⊤).inv.hom ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom r)) = r.
809	  -- Build the key categorical identity:
810	  --   (ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫ (hU.fromSpec.appIso ⊤).inv ≫
811	  --     Y.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op = 𝟙 _.
812	  have e₀ : (⊤ : (Spec Γ(Y, U)).Opens) ≤ hU.fromSpec ⁻¹ᵁ U :=
813	    le_of_eq hU.fromSpec_preimage_self.symm
814	  -- Sub-key: `hU.fromSpec.appLE U ⊤ e₀ = (ΓSpecIso _).inv` (via fromSpec_app_self).
815	  have h_appLE : hU.fromSpec.appLE U ⊤ e₀ = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso Γ(Y, U)).inv := by
816	    simp [Scheme.Hom.appLE, hU.fromSpec_app_self, ← Functor.map_comp]
817	  -- Apply `appLE_appIso_inv` and combine.
818	  have h_combine :
819	      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso Γ(Y, U)).inv ≫ (hU.fromSpec.appIso ⊤).inv =
820	        Y.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_of_eq hImg)).op := by
821	    rw [← h_appLE]
822	    exact Scheme.Hom.appLE_appIso_inv hU.fromSpec e₀
823	  -- Post-compose with `Y.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op` to collapse to 𝟙.
824	  have h_key :
825	      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso Γ(Y, U)).inv ≫ (hU.fromSpec.appIso ⊤).inv ≫
826	        Y.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op = 𝟙 _ := by
827	    rw [← Category.assoc, h_combine, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp]

829	    -- by `Subsingleton` of the poset structure on `Opens Y`.
830	    simp
831	  -- Apply h_key elementwise to r.
832	  exact congr($h_key r)

834	/-- **Section-level LinearEquiv via the Tilde route** (iter-188 Lane F NAMED
835	HELPER, iter-189 unbundling refactor).
836	
837	The substantive transport-and-intertwining helper: given a morphism `g : Y ⟶ X`
838	of schemes, a quasi-coherent module `N` on `X`, and affine opens
839	`V ⊆ X`, `U ⊆ Y` with `U ⊆ g⁻¹ V`, produces:
840	- a `Γ(Y, U)`-linear equiv between `TensorProduct Γ(X, V) Γ(Y, U) Γ(N, V)`
841	  and `Γ((pullback g).obj N, U)`, and
842	- a proof that this equiv sends `1 ⊗ x` to `pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap g N e x`
843	  (the Beck-Chevalley compatibility).
844	
845	The construction follows the iter-187 analogist-licensed Tilde route
846	(`analogies/quotscheme-isbasechange-tilde.md`):
847	  Step 1: identify `N|_V ≅ tilde Γ(N, V)` on `Spec Γ(X, V)` using
848	    `[N.IsQuasicoherent]` (extract a presentation on the affine open
849	    after transporting via `hV.isoSpec`).
850	  Step 2: pull back via `Spec.map φ : Spec Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Spec Γ(X, V)`,
851	    where `φ = g.appLE V U e`; apply `pullback_tildeIso` to obtain
852	    `(pullback (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde Γ(N, V)) ≅
853	      tilde (Γ(Y, U) ⊗ Γ(N, V))` on `Spec Γ(Y, U)`.
854	  Step 3: transport via `hU.isoSpec` back to `U`-sections of
855	    `(pullback g).obj N`.
856	  Step 4: evaluate at `⊤` via `tilde.isoTop` to extract the section-level
857	    linear equiv.
858	  Step 5: verify the intertwining via naturality of the adjunction unit
859	    (the Beck-Chevalley compatibility check; ~30-50 LOC).
860	
861	The substantive Mathlib gap content (Stacks 01HQ "pullback of tilde =
862	tilde of base change", plus the affine-open / Spec transport) is
863	factored into the present helper's body as a typed sorry. Once
864	`pullback_tildeIso` lands axiom-clean (iter-189+ sub-build) and the
865	transport infrastructure is in place, this helper closes axiom-clean
866	in ~30-50 LOC. -/
867	private theorem pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_sectionLinearEquiv
868	    {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X) (N : X.Modules) [N.IsQuasicoherent]
869	    {U : Y.Opens} {V : X.Opens}
870	    (_hU : IsAffineOpen U) (_hV : IsAffineOpen V)
871	    (e : U ≤ g ⁻¹ᵁ V) :
872	    letI : Algebra Γ(X, V) Γ(Y, U) := (g.appLE V U e).hom.toAlgebra
873	    letI : Module Γ(X, V) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj N, U) :=
874	      Module.compHom _ (g.appLE V U e).hom
875	    Nonempty {f : TensorProduct Γ(X, V) Γ(Y, U) Γ(N, V) ≃ₗ[Γ(Y, U)]
876	                Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj N, U) //
877	      ∀ x : Γ(N, V),
878	        f (1 ⊗ₜ[Γ(X, V)] x) = pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap g N e x} := by
879	  letI : Algebra Γ(X, V) Γ(Y, U) := (g.appLE V U e).hom.toAlgebra
880	  letI : Module Γ(X, V) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj N, U) :=
881	    Module.compHom _ (g.appLE V U e).hom
882	  -- iter-189 Lane F unbundle (per `analogies/lane-f-isbasechange.md`
883	  -- Decision 4): three Mathlib gaps are now pinned as separately-named
884	  -- typed sorries; the body of `_sectionLinearEquiv` is reduced to
885	  -- compositional bookkeeping over the chain.
886	  --
887	  -- Step 1 (Stacks 01I8 — `tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen`):
888	  --   `N|_{Spec Γ(X, V)} ≅ tilde Γ(N, V)`  on  `Spec Γ(X, V)`.
889	  -- Pulling back along `Spec.map φ : Spec Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Spec Γ(X, V)`
890	  -- (where `φ = g.appLE V U e`) and applying Step 2 (`pullback_tildeIso`,
891	  -- Stacks 01HQ) gives `(Spec.map φ)^* tilde Γ(N, V) ≅
892	  --   tilde (Γ(Y, U) ⊗_{Γ(X, V)} Γ(N, V))`.
893	  -- Identifying the two compositions via the commutative square
894	  -- `hU.fromSpec ≫ g = Spec.map φ ≫ hV.fromSpec` and applying Step 3
895	  -- transport (`pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict`) brings the
896	  -- section back to `U` itself. Evaluating tilde at `⊤` via
897	  -- `tilde.isoTop` extracts the section-level data; the underlying
898	  -- module of `tilde (Γ(Y, U) ⊗ Γ(N, V))` at `⊤` is exactly
899	  -- `Γ(Y, U) ⊗_{Γ(X, V)} Γ(N, V)`. The intertwining at `1 ⊗ x` (the
900	  -- Beck-Chevalley check) follows from naturality of the adjunction
901	  -- unit `pullback_app_isoTensor_unitAtV`.
902	  obtain ⟨⟨step1, _step1_apply⟩⟩ :=
903	    tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen N _hV
904	  obtain ⟨⟨step2, _step2_apply⟩⟩ :=
905	    pullback_tildeIso (g.appLE V U e) (ModuleCat.of Γ(X, V) Γ(N, V))
906	  obtain ⟨step3⟩ :=
907	    pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict
908	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj N) _hU
909	  -- iter-193 Lane F: assemble the iso chain at the sheaf level.
910	  -- The commutative square `hU.fromSpec ≫ g = Spec.map φ ≫ hV.fromSpec`
911	  -- (where `φ = g.appLE V U e`) comes from Mathlib's
912	  -- `IsAffineOpen.SpecMap_appLE_fromSpec`.
913	  have h_eq : _hU.fromSpec ≫ g = Spec.map (g.appLE V U e) ≫ _hV.fromSpec :=
914	    (IsAffineOpen.SpecMap_appLE_fromSpec g _hV _hU e).symm
915	  -- Sheaf-level iso chain (5-step compositional transport):
916	  --   (pullback hU.fromSpec).obj ((pullback g).obj N)
917	  -- = (pullback g ⋙ pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N                         [defeq]
918	  -- ≅ (pullback (hU.fromSpec ≫ g)).obj N             [pullbackComp]
919	  -- ≅ (pullback (Spec.map φ ≫ hV.fromSpec)).obj N    [pullbackCongr h_eq]
920	  -- ≅ (pullback (Spec.map φ)).obj ((pullback hV.fromSpec).obj N)
921	  --                                                   [(pullbackComp).symm]
922	  -- ≅ (pullback (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde Γ(N, V))    [step1 (Stacks 01I8)]
923	  -- ≅ tilde (TensorProduct Γ(X,V) Γ(Y,U) Γ(N,V))     [step2 (Stacks 01HQ)]
924	  let composedIso :=
925	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp _hU.fromSpec g).app N ≪≫
926	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h_eq).app N ≪≫
927	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (Spec.map (g.appLE V U e)) _hV.fromSpec).app N).symm ≪≫
928	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map (g.appLE V U e))).mapIso step1 ≪≫
929	      step2)
930	  -- iter-193 Lane F partial: the AddEquiv from sheaf-level `composedIso` at
931	  -- ⊤-sections is established below. The remaining residual (iter-194+) is:
932	  -- (a) chain `topAdd` with `tilde.isoTop.symm` to land in TensorProduct;
933	  -- (b) upgrade AddEquiv → Γ(Y, U)-LinearEquiv via Hom.app_smul + ΓSpecIso;
934	  -- (c) compose with `step3` to reach Γ((pullback g).obj N, U);
935	  -- (d) verify the Beck-Chevalley intertwining `1 ⊗ x ↦ baseMap g N e x`
936	  --     using naturality of the adjunction unit `pullback_app_isoTensor_unitAtV`.
937	  let topAdd :=
938	    { toFun := fun x => (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app composedIso.hom ⊤).hom x
939	      invFun := fun y => (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app composedIso.inv ⊤).hom y
940	      left_inv := fun x => by
941	        simp only [← AddCommGrpCat.comp_apply,
942	          ← Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app, composedIso.hom_inv_id,
943	          Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app, AddCommGrpCat.hom_id, AddMonoidHom.id_apply]
944	      right_inv := fun y => by
945	        simp only [← AddCommGrpCat.comp_apply,
946	          ← Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app, composedIso.inv_hom_id,
947	          Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app, AddCommGrpCat.hom_id, AddMonoidHom.id_apply]
948	      map_add' := fun x y =>
949	        (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app composedIso.hom ⊤).hom.map_add x y

11	# The line bundle `𝒪_C(P)` of a closed point and its global sections (RR.3)
12	
13	This file is the **RR.3** sub-build chapter for the project's headline
14	`genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geometrically irreducible
15	genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma in
16	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`).
17	
18	The Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain for the genus-`0` ↦ `ℙ¹` classification routes
19	through:
20	
21	- `RR.1` (`WeilDivisor.lean`): the Weil divisor group `Div(C)` and the
22	  degree map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ`.
23	- `RR.2` (`RRFormula.lean`): the Euler-characteristic identity
24	  `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g` and the Riemann–Roch dimension formula
25	  `ℓ(D) = deg(D) + 1` in genus `0`.
26	- **`RR.3` (this file)**: the invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(P)` of a closed point
27	  `P ∈ C`, its `k̄`-vector space of global sections as the
28	  Riemann–Roch space `L([P])`, the `H¹`-vanishing
29	  `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 0` on a genus-`0` curve, the dimension formula
30	  `dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2`, and the existence of a non-constant
31	  rational function `f ∈ K(C)` with at most a simple pole at `P`.
32	- `RR.4` (`RationalCurveIso.lean`, future): the "two-section
33	  ⇒ `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` ⇒ `≅ ℙ¹`" classification.
34

51	   (L154) remain typed `sorry` for iter-184+ (the full chain

56	The 5 pinned declarations are:
57	
58	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint` — the invertible
59	   sheaf `𝒪_C(P)` associated to a closed point `P` on a smooth proper
60	   curve `C / k̄`.
61	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.globalSections_iff`
62	   — the identification of global sections of `𝒪_C(P)` with the
63	   Riemann–Roch space
64	   `L([P]) = {f ∈ K(C)^× | div(f) + [P] ≥ 0} ∪ {0}`,
65	   expressed as an `Iff`-style characterisation of the order conditions
66	   `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for `Q ≠ P` and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`.
67	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.h1_vanishing_genusZero`
68	   — the cohomological vanishing `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 0` on a smooth proper
69	   geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄` with `g(C) = 0`, via the long
70	   exact sequence of the closed-point short exact sequence
71	   `0 → 𝒪_C → 𝒪_C(P) → k(P) → 0`.
72	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.dim_eq_two_of_genusZero`
73	   — the dimension formula `dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2` in genus `0`,
74	   specialising the χ-identity `RR.2` to `D = [P]` and consuming the
75	   `H¹`-vanishing of pin 3.
76	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.exists_nonconstant_genusZero`
77	   — the corollary: a non-constant rational function `f ∈ K(C)` regular
78	   on `C \ {P}` with at most a simple pole at `P`, obtained as a lift of
79	   any non-zero element of the quotient
80	   `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) / 𝓀̄ · 1`.

112	/-! ## §1. The line bundle of a closed point on a smooth proper curve
113	
114	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄` and a closed
115	point `P ∈ C`, the local ring `𝒪_{C,P}` is a DVR with maximal ideal
116	generated by a uniformiser `f_P`. Hartshorne's construction `ℒ(D)`
117	(II §6 p. 144) applied to the Cartier divisor `[P]` (locally cut out by
118	`f_P` near `P`, by `1` elsewhere) produces the invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(P)`:
119	the sub-`𝒪_C`-module of the function-field constant sheaf `𝒦_C ≅ K(C)`
120	generated locally by `f_P^{-1}` near `P` and by `1` on `C \ {P}`. -/
121	
122	namespace Scheme
123	
124	/-! ### Hartshorne subsheaf-of-`K_C` carrier (iter-183 Lane A scaffold)
125	
126	The substantive iter-183 contribution: a concrete per-open `Set`-valued
127	carrier of the line bundle `𝒪_C(P)`, realised directly as Hartshorne's
128	subsheaf of the function-field constant sheaf `K_C` (Hartshorne II §6
129	p. 144; analogist `ocofp-sheaf-internalhom.md` Decision 3
130	`ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB`).
131	
132	A section of `𝒪_C(P)` over an open `U` is a rational function `f ∈ K(C)`
133	satisfying the order conditions `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor
134	`Q ≠ P` with `Q.point ∈ U` (regularity on the complement of `P` inside
135	`U`) and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` when `P ∈ U` (at most a simple pole at `P`).
136	The construction is independent of the choice of uniformiser at `P`:
137	any two uniformisers differ by a unit, so the order-`≥ −1` condition at
138	`P` is intrinsic.
139	
140	iter-184+ will upgrade `carrierSet` to a `Submodule kbar K(C)` via the
141	closure proofs (zero / addition / kbar-scalar), bundle the result as a
142	presheaf functor (identity-on-`K(C)` restrictions, monotone in `U` via
143	`carrierSet_mono`), and discharge the sheaf property via gluing-by-
144	stalks (stalk-locality of the order conditions at each prime divisor). -/
145	
146	/-- **Carrier set** of `𝒪_C(P)` over an open `U : (Opens C.left)ᵒᵖ`: the
147	set of rational functions `f ∈ K(C)` satisfying the order conditions
148	`ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q` with `Q.point ∈ U.unop`,
149	`Q.point ≠ P`, and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` when `P ∈ U.unop`.
150	
151	iter-183 Lane A landed this as a concrete substantive `Set` definition;
152	no sorry. -/
153	private noncomputable def lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet
154	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
155	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsIntegral C.left]
156	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
157	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
158	    (P : C.left) (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
159	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
160	    Set C.left.functionField := by
161	  let Phat : C.left.PrimeDivisor := ⟨P, hPcoh⟩
162	  haveI := Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.instKrullDimLEStalk
163	    (X := C.left) Phat
164	  exact { f | (∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Q.point ∈ U.unop.1 → Q.point ≠ P →
165	              0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f) ∧
166	              (P ∈ U.unop.1 → (-1 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Phat f) }
167	
168	/-- **Monotonicity of `carrierSet` in `U`**: when `V.unop ⊆ U.unop` (i.e.
169	the open `V` is contained in the open `U`), the carrier on `U` is
170	INCLUDED in the carrier on `V` (the order conditions over the smaller
171	open `V` involve fewer prime divisors, hence are easier to satisfy).
172	
173	This is the substantive monotonicity that drives the (identity-on-`K(C)`)
174	restriction map of the would-be `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.presheaf` functor:
175	in `(Opens C.left)ᵒᵖ`, an arrow `U ⟶ V` corresponds to `V.unop ⊆ U.unop`,
176	and the restriction map `carrierSet U → carrierSet V` is the inclusion
177	delivered by this lemma.
178	
179	iter-183 Lane A landed this as the substantive monotonicity proof; no
180	sorry. -/
181	private lemma lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet_mono
182	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
183	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsIntegral C.left]
184	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
185	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
186	    (P : C.left) (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
187	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ}
188	    (hUV : V.unop.1 ⊆ U.unop.1) :
189	    lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet P hPcoh U
190	      ⊆ lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet P hPcoh V := by
191	  intro f hf
192	  refine ⟨fun Q hQV hQP => hf.1 Q (hUV hQV) hQP, fun hPV => hf.2 (hUV hPV)⟩
193	
194	/-- **Nonempty top-open**: for an integral scheme `X`, the top open is
195	nonempty (its carrier is `Set.univ` and `X` itself is nonempty since
196	`IsIntegral X ⟹ IrreducibleSpace X ⟹ Nonempty X`). Supplied as an
197	instance so that the standard Mathlib `Algebra Γ(X, U) X.functionField`
198	instance fires at `U = ⊤`. -/
199	instance lineBundleAtClosedPoint.instNonemptyTopOpen
200	    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] :

1200	substrate in `Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf` (`OcOfD.lean` — STRUCTURALLY
1201	BLOCKED, standing deferral) and `Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_*`
1202	(`RRFormula.lean` — gated on the χ-additivity helper). This typed sorry
1203	is the natural single point at which all those upstream gates flow into
1204	the `dim_eq_two_of_genusZero` close.

1206	Blueprint reference: `thm:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_dim_eq_two_of_genusZero`
1207	(Hartshorne IV.1 Example 1.3.5, p. 297) — the chapter's "RR.2-route"

1210	    (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
1211	    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
1212	    (_hg : AlgebraicGeometry.genus C = 0) :
1213	    (Module.finrank kbar

1217	        (Scheme.HModule kbar

1221	/-- **The dimension formula `dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2` on a smooth
1222	proper geometrically irreducible genus-`0` curve over `k̄`**
1223	(Hartshorne IV §1 Example 1.3.5, p. 297).
1224	
1225	iter-177+ body: invoke
1226	`Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus` on the
1227	`ModuleCat k̄`-valued sheaf `lineBundleAtClosedPoint P hP` (matching the

1233	`(Module.finrank kbar H⁰) − (Module.finrank kbar H¹)`, substitute
1234	`Module.finrank kbar H¹ = 0` from `h1_vanishing_genusZero`, and read

1237	Blueprint reference: `thm:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_dim_eq_two_of_genusZero`
1238	(Hartshorne IV.1 Example 1.3.5, p. 297).

1240	iter-194 Lane A first body push: the body is now mechanical arithmetic
1241	(finrank H⁰ - finrank H¹ = 2 with H¹ = 0 gives H⁰ = 2) on top of two
1242	named substrate helpers:
1243	* `h1_vanishing_genusZero` (already in this file) — supplies finrank H¹ = 0.
1244	* `h0_sub_h1_lineBundleAtClosedPoint_eq_two` (named helper above) —

1247	theorem dim_eq_two_of_genusZero
1248	    (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
1249	    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
1250	    (_hg : AlgebraicGeometry.genus C = 0) :
1251	    Module.finrank kbar
1252	        (Scheme.HModule kbar
1253	          (lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP hPcoh) 0) = 2 := by
1254	  -- Step 1: H¹(𝒪_C(P)) = 0 via the genus-0 SES.
1255	  have hH1 :
1256	      Module.finrank kbar
1257	        (Scheme.HModule kbar
1258	          (lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP hPcoh) 1) = 0 :=
1259	    h1_vanishing_genusZero P hP hPcoh _hg
1260	  -- Step 2: `(H⁰ : ℤ) − (H¹ : ℤ) = 2` via the RR.2 χ-identity at `D = [P]`.
1261	  have hχ := h0_sub_h1_lineBundleAtClosedPoint_eq_two P hP hPcoh _hg
1262	  -- Step 3: substitute `H¹ = 0`, read off `H⁰ = 2` via `Int → Nat`.
1263	  rw [hH1, Nat.cast_zero, sub_zero] at hχ
1264	  exact_mod_cast hχ

1266	/-! ## §5. A non-constant rational function with at most a simple pole at `P`
1267	
1268	The two-dimensionality of `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))` and the one-dimensional
1269	constant subspace `k̄ · 1` give a non-zero quotient `H⁰/k̄`. Any lift
1270	of a non-zero element of the quotient is, under the identification of
1271	`globalSections_iff`, a non-constant rational function `f ∈ K(C)` with
1272	the order conditions `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for `Q ≠ P` and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`.
1273	This is the seed of `RR.4` (the morphism `C → ℙ¹` produced by
1274	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` from the basis `(1, f)`). -/
1275	
1276	/-- **Existence of a non-constant rational function with the order
1277	conditions and nonzero principal divisor, from `dim H⁰(𝒪_C(P)) = 2`.**
1278	
1279	iter-194 Lane A first body push — named substrate helper carving the
1280	linear-algebra + principal-divisor content out of
1281	`exists_nonconstant_genusZero` (so the body of the latter is a one-line
1282	invocation supplied with the dimension count from `dim_eq_two_of_genusZero`).
1283	
1284	The helper carries the substantive content independent of the genus
1285	hypothesis (only the dim H⁰ = 2 input is consumed):
1286	
1287	1. **Linear algebra.** The structural inclusion of constants
1288	   `kbar →ₗ[kbar] H⁰(𝒪_C(P))` (`LinearMap.toSpanSingleton` applied to the
1289	   distinguished section `s₁` built from `globalSections_iff_mp` at
1290	   `f = 1` with the trivial order conditions
1291	   `(∀ Q ≠ P, 0 ≤ ord_Q 1 = 0) ∧ (-1 ≤ ord_P 1 = 0)`) has 1-dimensional
1292	   image, so by `hdim : dim H⁰ = 2 > 1` the image is a strict subspace and
1293	   a section `s ∈ H⁰ ∖ kbar · s₁` exists.
1294	2. **Function-field extraction.** Set `f := toFunctionField P hP hPcoh s`,
1295	   non-zero because `s ∉ kbar · s₁` and `toFunctionField` is kbar-linear
1296	   on the constant subspace (the linearity ensures
1297	   `s ∈ kbar · s₁ ⟺ f ∈ kbar · 1 = image of algebraMap kbar K(C)`).
1298	3. **Order conditions.** Apply `globalSections_iff_mpr P hP f hf hPcoh ⟨s, rfl⟩`
1299	   to read off the pair `(∀ Q ≠ P, 0 ≤ ord_Q f) ∧ (-1 ≤ ord_P f)`.
1300	4. **Principal divisor non-vanishing.** Use the Stacks 02P0 / Hartshorne
1301	   II.6.7 statement: a rational function `f ∈ K(C)` with `principal f = 0`
1302	   (no zeros or poles at any prime divisor) is a global unit
1303	   `f ∈ Γ(C, 𝒪_C)^× = k̄^×` on a smooth proper geometrically irreducible
1304	   curve over `k̄`. Contrapositive: `f ∉ k̄` ⟹ `principal f ≠ 0`. The
1305	   non-constancy from step 2 supplies the hypothesis.
1306	
1307	Substrate gates:
1308	- Step 1's linear-algebra extraction is mechanical from `hdim` once the
1309	  constant-inclusion `kbar → H⁰` is set up (and the `s₁` distinguished
1310	  section exists via `globalSections_iff_mp`).
1311	- Step 4's principal-divisor non-vanishing is the standard
1312	  Hartshorne~II.6.7 / Stacks 02P0 argument; it consumes properness +
1313	  integrality of `C.left` and the function-field identification of
1314	  `Γ(C, 𝒪_C^×) ≅ k̄^×` from `IsAlgClosed kbar`.
1315	
1316	This helper is the natural single point at which the `exists_nonconstant_genusZero`
1317	substantive content lives, isolated from the dimension-count hypothesis
1318	(which is itself isolated in `dim_eq_two_of_genusZero` ↦
1319	`h0_sub_h1_lineBundleAtClosedPoint_eq_two` ↦ `h1_vanishing_genusZero`).
1320	
1321	Blueprint reference: `cor:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_exists_nonconstant_genusZero`
1322	(Hartshorne IV.1 Exercise 1.1, p. 297). -/

1116	`0 → 𝒪_C(−[P]) → 𝒪_C → k(P) → 0` (Hartshorne II.6.18: the ideal sheaf
1117	of the locally principal closed subscheme `P` is `𝒪_C(−[P])`; the
1118	quotient is the skyscraper `k(P) ≅ k̄` at `P`) tensored by the locally
1119	free rank-`1` sheaf `𝒪_C([P])` (left rigid, so preserves exactness and
1120	leaves the skyscraper invariant) becomes
1121	`0 → 𝒪_C → 𝒪_C(P) → k(P) → 0` in `Coh(C)`. The associated long exact
1122	sequence of sheaf cohomology, combined with `H¹(C, 𝒪_C) = 0`
1123	(genus-`0` hypothesis: `g(C) = dim_{k̄} H¹(C, 𝒪_C)`) and
1124	`H¹(C, k(P)) = 0` (skyscraper / flasque), kills `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P))`. -/

1127	irreducible curve of genus `0`** (Hartshorne IV §1 p. 296, the
1128	inductive step of Theorem 1.3 specialised to `D = 0`).

1130	Concretely, the finite-dimensional `k̄`-vector space

1132	`0`, i.e. is the trivial vector space.

10	/-!
11	# The rational-curve isomorphism `C ≅ ℙ¹` (RR.4)
12	
13	This file is the **RR.4** sub-build chapter for the project's headline
14	`genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geometrically irreducible
15	genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma pinned in
16	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity:290`).
17	
18	The Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain for the genus-`0` ⇒ `ℙ¹` classification routes
19	through the four sub-build chapters:
20	
21	- `RR.1` (`WeilDivisor.lean`): the Weil divisor group `Div(C)`, the degree
22	  map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ`, and the degree-zero principal-divisor identity.
23	- `RR.2` (`RRFormula.lean`): the Euler-characteristic identity
24	  `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g` and Riemann–Roch in genus zero.
25	- `RR.3` (`OCofP.lean`): the line bundle `𝒪_C([P])` of a closed point and
26	  the existence of a non-constant rational function
27	  `f ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C([P])) ∖ k̄ · 1`.
28	- **`RR.4` (this file)**: the global classification — the linear-system
29	  morphism `C ⟶ ℙ¹` produced from `(1, f)` (Pin 1), its degree identified
30	  via the pole divisor (Pin 2), and the degree-`1` ⇒ isomorphism step
31	  (Pin 3). The headline target `AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero_curve_iso_P1`
32	  (Pin 4) is the existing pinned declaration at
33	  `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:290` — see §0 below.
34	
35	## Status (iter-177 file-skeleton; iter-181 Pin 3 signature refinement)
36	
37	This file was originally the **iter-177 Lane 8** file-skeleton. Each
38	of the three new pinned declarations carries the *intended*
39	substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
40	`blueprint/src/chapters/RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso.tex`) with a
41	`sorry` body. Bodies are iter-182+ work after the RR.1/RR.2/RR.3
42	chain matures (in particular `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero`
43	and the `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C([P]))`-non-constant existence corollary of `RR.3`).
44	
45	iter-181 Lane I refined the Pin 3 signature of
46	`iso_of_degree_one`: the abstract function-field-iso existence
47	hypothesis is replaced by the `[Algebra K(C') K(C)]` typeclass binder
48	+ `Module.finrank K(C') K(C) = 1` equation, matching what the
49	iter-182+ body needs (per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md`
50	Decision 2 DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY).
51	
52	The 3 new pinned declarations are:
53	
54	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphismToP1OfGlobalSections` — from a
55	   graded `k̄`-algebra hom `k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)` whose image of the
56	   irrelevant ideal generates the unit ideal, produce the morphism
57	   `X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` via `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` lifted to
58	   the slice category over `Spec k̄`.
59	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor` — for a
60	   non-constant morphism `φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` from a smooth
61	   proper curve, extract a positive-degree Weil divisor on `C` (the
62	   pole divisor `φ^*[∞]`).
63	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.iso_of_degree_one` — a non-constant
64	   morphism between smooth proper geometrically irreducible curves
65	   whose induced function-field map is a `k̄`-algebra iso is an
66	   isomorphism of schemes (Hartshorne I.6.12 specialised to
67	   degree-`1`).
68	
69	The headline pin (Pin 4) is satisfied by the existing AVR.lean target.
70	
71	## Note on type expressivity
72	
73	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
74	each pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
75	
76	* `morphismToP1OfGlobalSections` returns a *concrete* morphism
77	  `X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` of `Over (Spec k̄)`, not `Iso.refl _` or a
78	  reflexive placeholder; the input data is the exact pair
79	  `(f, hf)` consumed by Mathlib's `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`.
80	* `morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor` asserts the existence of a
81	  positive-integer degree `d` together with a Weil divisor `D` on `C`
82	  realising it — the *named substantive content* of the degree-via-pole
83	  identification. Unfolds to `∃ d D, 0 < d ∧ D.degree = d`, not `True`.
84	* `iso_of_degree_one` asserts `Nonempty (C ≅ C')` from the
85	  non-constant hypothesis together with the *substantive* degree-`1`
86	  hypothesis `Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField = 1`
87	  (paired with an `[Algebra C'.functionField C.functionField]` typeclass
88	  binder for the canonical `φ`-induced function-field map). The
89	  `Nonempty`-wrapped iso is the exact downstream-consumer shape,
90	  matching the `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` headline signature in AVR.lean.
91	  This signature was refined in iter-181 Lane I from the iter-177
92	  file-skeleton placeholder `Nonempty (C'.ff ≃+* C.ff)` to make the
93	  degree-`1`-induced-by-`φ` hypothesis the substantive content; see
94	  `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Decision 2.
95	
96	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
97	(the concrete `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`-derived morphism, the
98	existence-of-positive-degree-divisor statement, the existence of a
99	scheme isomorphism); no `Iso.refl _` / empty-content `proof_wanted` /
100	`Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` placeholders are used.
101	
102	## Curve conventions
103	
104	Following the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`,
105	`Jacobian.lean`, `OCofP.lean`), a **smooth proper geometrically
106	irreducible curve over `k̄`** carries:
107	
108	- `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]`,
109	- `[IsProper C.hom]`,
110	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]`.
111	
112	These instances are supplied by `inferInstance` at the call site. The
113	`[IsIntegral C.left]` / `[IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]` instances
114	required by the Weil-divisor / `RationalMap.order` API are threaded
115	explicitly per-lemma where consumed.
116

125	`ℙⁿ` via global sections). Stacks Project tag `0AVX` (degree-`1`

166	(`morphismToP1OfGlobalSections`)
167	
168	Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1, specialised to `ℙ¹` over `k̄`. Given a graded
169	`k̄`-algebra homomorphism `f : k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)` whose image of the
170	irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)` generates the unit ideal of `Γ(X, ⊤)`, the
171	construction `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of Mathlib's
172	`AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic` produces a morphism
173	`X.left ⟶ Proj 𝒜 = ProjectiveLineBarScheme k̄`. Lifting to the slice
174	category over `Spec k̄` (via `Over.homMk` with a section-condition
175	proof) yields the pinned morphism
176	`X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄ : Over (Spec (.of kbar))`. -/
177	
178	/-- **The morphism `X ⟶ ℙ¹_{k̄}` produced from a globally-generating pair of
179	homogeneous sections** (Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1; Mathlib's
180	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` specialised to the standard
181	ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`).
182	
183	Given a graded `k̄`-algebra homomorphism `f : k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)`, a
184	`k̄`-algebra compatibility witness `halg` (recording that the composite
185	`kbar → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar → Γ(X.left, ⊤)` agrees with the natural
186	algebra map `kbar → Γ(X.left, ⊤)` induced by `X.hom`), and a proof `hf`
187	that the image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁) ⊆ k̄[X₀, X₁]` generates
188	the unit ideal of `Γ(X, ⊤)`, this constructs the unique morphism
189	`φ : X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` characterised on basic-opens by
190	`φ ⁻¹ᵁ D₊(X_i) = X.basicOpen (f X_i)` (the "no-common-zero" expression of
191	Hartshorne's morphism condition). The composite
192	`φ.left ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ = X.hom`, witnessing the `Over (Spec k̄)`
193	section condition.
194	
195	The input data `(f, halg, hf)` is the standard Hartshorne II §7 packaging
196	of "a pair of global sections `s₀, s₁ ∈ H⁰(X, ℒ)` of an invertible sheaf
197	`ℒ` without common zeros": `f` is the graded `k̄`-algebra map sending
198	`X_i ↦ s_i ∈ Γ(X, ℒ) ⊂ Γ(X, ⊤)` (after a choice of trivialisation
199	identifying `H⁰(X, ℒ)` with a submodule of `Γ(X, ⊤)` over the affine
200	cover `X = D(s₀) ∪ D(s₁)`); `halg` is the `kbar`-algebra-compatibility
201	condition (without which the section condition over `Spec kbar` cannot
202	be derived); `hf` is the no-common-zero condition.
203	
204	The construction invokes `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` on the standard

209	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` plus

211	`X.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp MvPolynomial.C)) = X.hom`,

216	Blueprint reference: `lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections`
217	(Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1, II §7). -/

407	/-- **iter-195 Lane RCI structural lift** — uniformiser witness for the
408	local parameter `(localParameterAtInfty kbar).val` at the prime divisor
409	`Y₀ = [∞] ∈ Div(ℙ¹_{kbar})`.
410	
411	The rational function `t = X 0 / X 1 ∈ K(ℙ¹)` (`localParameterAtInfty kbar`
412	in this file) is a uniformiser at the "point at infinity"
413	`Y₀ = [∞] ∈ Div(ℙ¹)`, i.e. it satisfies `ord_{Y₀}(t) = 1` and is
414	non-vanishing at every other prime divisor. Equivalently, the zero
415	divisor `(t)_0 = [Y₀]` on `ℙ¹` has degree `1`.
416	
417	This is the uniformiser hypothesis required by
418	`Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` (the
419	Hartshorne~II.6.9 statement) at the consumer site
420	`Hom.poleDivisor_degree_eq_finrank`. iter-195 lifts the iter-194
421	inline typed sorry (`?hLPUnif`) at the consumer to this named, file-
422	private helper so the substrate need is documented at a single
423	named declaration rather than buried inline in the consumer body.
424	
425	**Closure path (iter-196+; 3-step substantive close).**

478	/-- **The pole divisor of a morphism `φ : C → ℙ¹` of smooth proper curves
479	over `k̄`.** As a Weil divisor on `C`, it is `φ^*[∞]` — the pullback of the
480	divisor `[∞]` on `ℙ¹` along `φ`.
481	
482	**iter-190 substantive body (Pin 2 corrective Option (a) — positive part of
483	principal).** The body is the *positive part* of the principal Weil divisor
484	on `C` of the pulled-back local parameter
485	`algebraMap K(ℙ¹) K(C) (localParameterAtInfty kbar).val`:
486	
487	  `Hom.poleDivisor φ := positivePart (principal (algebraMap K(ℙ¹) K(C) t_∞) _)`
488	
489	Mathematically, `div(φ^* t_∞) = (φ^* t_∞)_0 − (φ^* t_∞)_∞` on `C`, where
490	`(φ^* t_∞)_0 = φ^*[∞]` (the zeros of `φ^* t_∞` are exactly the preimages
491	of `∞`, where `t_∞` vanishes on `ℙ¹`) and `(φ^* t_∞)_∞ = φ^*[0]` (the
492	poles are the preimages of `0`). Extracting the positive part of
493	`div(φ^* t_∞)` exactly recovers `φ^*[∞]` on the nose.
494	
495	**Why the positive-part wrap is essential (Pin 2 structural-conflict
496	diagnosis, iter-189 → iter-190 corrective).** The naked principal divisor
497	`principal (algebraMap _ _ t_∞) halg` has degree zero on a complete
498	nonsingular curve (Hartshorne II.6.10 / Stacks 0BE3,
499	`Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero` of `WeilDivisor.lean`),
500	whereas the RHS `Module.finrank K(ℙ¹) K(C)` of
501	`Hom.poleDivisor_degree_eq_finrank` is positive (= deg φ ≥ 1) at every
502	call site that uses the φ-induced algebra structure. The positive-part
503	wrap restores provability: `degree (positivePart (principal (φ^# t_∞)))
504	= degree (φ^*[∞]) = deg(φ) = [K(C):K(ℙ¹)]` (Hartshorne~II.6.9).
505	
506	The φ-dependence is carried by the `[Algebra K(ℙ¹) K(C)]` instance
507	binder, which at every call site is the canonical `φ`-induced
508	function-field map (per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Decision 2).
509	Changing the algebra instance changes the divisor; in particular, the
510	unfolding
511	`Hom.poleDivisor φ ⇝ positivePart (principal (algebraMap _ _ t) _)`
512	is visible to definitional unfolding, *not* opaque behind
513	`Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / empty `proof_wanted` placeholders.
514	
515	**Helper budget**: 1 named typed sorry (`localParameterAtInfty`, the
516	local-parameter choice; substrate gap on the chart-`1` ratio coordinate
517	of `projectiveLineBarAffineCover`). The Pin 2 corrective additionally
518	introduces a file-local `WeilDivisor.positivePart` def + named typed-sorry
519	pin `WeilDivisor.degree_positivePart_principal_localParameterAtInfty_eq_finrank`
520	above; these are scheduled to migrate to the public
521	`Scheme.WeilDivisor.positivePart` /
522	`Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` of
523	`WeilDivisor.lean` once the parallel iter-190 WeilDivisor prover lands
524	the public versions.

526	Blueprint reference: `def:Hom.poleDivisor` (Hartshorne IV §2 opening +
527	II §6.9; chapter `chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso` §2 "Convention on
528	the pole divisor"); iter-190 plan-phase Pin 2 corrective NOTE on
529	`lem:degree_via_pole_divisor`. -/

542	  -- canonical `φ`-induced algebra map, take its principal divisor, then
543	  -- extract the positive part (i.e. the zeros, which on a complete curve

549	      algebraMap (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
550	          C.left.functionField t.val ≠ 0 := by
551	    intro h

553	    have hinj :
554	        Function.Injective

560	            C.left.functionField 0 = 0 :=
561	      RingHom.map_zero _
562	    exact hinj (h.trans h0.symm)
563	  WeilDivisor.positivePart
564	    (Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal
565	      (algebraMap (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField

645	/-- **Degree of a non-constant morphism to `ℙ¹` via its pole divisor**
646	(Hartshorne IV §2 p. 299 opening + II §6.9 multiplicativity of degree
647	under finite pullback).
648	
649	For a non-constant morphism `φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` from a smooth
650	proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over an algebraically closed
651	field `k̄`, there exists a positive integer `d` (= `deg(φ)`) together
652	with a Weil divisor `D` on `C` (= the pole divisor `φ^*[∞]` of the
653	pulled-back affine coordinate) of total degree `d`.
654	
655	The non-constancy hypothesis is encoded as `∀ Q, φ ≠ toUnit C ≫ Q`
656	(no constant morphism `C ⟶ ℙ¹` agrees with `φ`); this is equivalent to
657	`φ` being surjective onto `ℙ¹_{k̄}` since the image is a closed
658	irreducible subset of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` of dimension ≥ 1, and the only such is
659	`ℙ¹_{k̄}` itself.
660	
661	iter-178+ body: a non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves
662	over `k̄` is automatically finite (proper + quasi-finite). The
663	function-field extension `k̄(ℙ¹) ↪ K(C)` is finite of some degree
664	`d = [K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)] ≥ 1`. Take `D := φ^*[∞] ∈ Div(C)` via the standard
665	"pullback of a Weil divisor along a finite morphism" operation; by
666	Hartshorne II.6.9, `deg(D) = deg(φ) · deg([∞]) = d · 1 = d`. The
667	positivity `d ≥ 1` follows from non-constancy (else `deg(φ) = 0` would
668	force `φ` constant). Both the finite-pullback operation and the
669	multiplicativity-of-degree identity are project-bespoke sub-lemmas
670	threaded inside the iter-178+ body (cf. the closely related
671	`Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero` of `RR.1`).
672	
673	Blueprint reference: `lem:degree_via_pole_divisor` (Hartshorne IV §2
674	opening, p. 299; II §6.9 p. 137 for multiplicativity).

678	flagged `weakened-wrong` by the iter-181 `lean-vs-blueprint-checker`:

681	`Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor φ` (a *specific* divisor depending on `φ`) and

687	(verdict PROCEED) for the body recipe via

915	/-! ## §7. Linear equivalence and the divisor class group -/
916	
917	/-- **Linear equivalence of Weil divisors.**
918	
919	Two Weil divisors `D, D' ∈ Div(X)` are linearly equivalent, written `D ~ D'`, if
920	and only if there exists a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×` with
921	`D - D' = div(f) ∈ Div(X)`.
922	
923	`~` is an equivalence relation (reflexivity from `div(1) = 0`, symmetry from
924	`div(f⁻¹) = -div(f)`, transitivity from `div(fg) = div(f) + div(g)`, all via
925	`thm:principal_hom`). The quotient `Cl(X) := Div(X) / im(div)` is the
926	**divisor class group** of `X`.
927	
928	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, `thm:principal_deg_zero` shows the
929	degree map descends to `deg : Cl(C) → ℤ`.
930	
931	Blueprint reference: `def:linear_equivalence` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 131). -/
932	def LinearEquivalence [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
933	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (D D' : X.WeilDivisor) : Prop :=
934	  ∃ (f : X.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0), D - D' = principal f hf
935	
936	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
937	
938	end AlgebraicGeometry
939

63	/-! ## §1. Codim-1 cycle group / Weil divisor group
64	
65	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` (II §6, p. 130) is: `X` is a Noetherian integral
66	separated scheme that is regular in codimension one. Under `(*)`, prime divisors
67	are closed integral subschemes of codimension one, and the local ring at the
68	generic point of a prime divisor is a DVR with quotient field the function field
69	`K(X)`.
70	
71	A prime divisor is encoded by its **generic point** together with the
72	codimension-one witness `Order.coheight point = 1`: the data field `point : X`
73	selects the generic point of the closed integral subscheme, and the predicate
74	field `coheight` enforces codimension one in the specialisation preorder
75	(Hartshorne II §6 p. 130; blueprint pin `def:prime_divisor`). Integrality of the
76	closure is automatic, so no separate integrality witness is needed.
77	-/
78	
79	/-- A **prime divisor** on a scheme `X`: a closed integral subscheme of codimension
80	one, encoded (for a scheme satisfying Hartshorne's condition `(*)`) by its generic
81	point. On a curve, prime divisors correspond bijectively to closed points.
82	
83	The codimension-one witness is the predicate `Order.coheight point = 1` on the
84	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier` (Mathlib's convention is
85	`x ≤ y ↔ y ⤳ x`, so the generic point is the unique maximal element and a
86	generic point of a codim-1 closed integral subscheme has a length-one chain
87	above it). The closure `{point}̄` is automatically irreducible (so the reduced
88	induced subscheme structure is automatically integral); hence no separate
89	integrality field is needed.
90	
91	Blueprint reference: `def:prime_divisor` / `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6
92	p. 130; Stacks 02RW). -/
93	structure Scheme.PrimeDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) where
94	  /-- The generic point of the closed integral subscheme. -/
95	  point : X
96	  /-- Codimension-1 witness: `point` has coheight `1` in the specialisation
97	  preorder on `X.carrier`. Per iter-173 `wd-spec-refine` (`def:prime_divisor`). -/
98	  coheight : Order.coheight point = 1
99	
100	/-- The **Weil divisor group** of a scheme `X` satisfying Hartshorne's condition
101	`(*)`: the free abelian group on the set of prime divisors of `X`,
102	`Div(X) = ⨁_{Y prime divisor of X} ℤ · Y`. An element `D = Σ nᵢ · Yᵢ` with finitely
103	many nonzero coefficients is a **Weil divisor**; if all `nᵢ ≥ 0` it is **effective**.
104	
105	Blueprint reference: `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 130). -/
106	def Scheme.WeilDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type u := X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ
107	
108	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
109	
110	noncomputable instance (X : Scheme.{u}) : AddCommGroup X.WeilDivisor :=
111	  inferInstanceAs (AddCommGroup (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ))
112	
113	instance (X : Scheme.{u}) : Inhabited X.WeilDivisor :=
114	  inferInstanceAs (Inhabited (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ))
115	
116	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
117	
118	/-! ## §2. Order of a rational function at a prime divisor
119	
120	For a scheme `X` satisfying `(*)`, every prime divisor `Y` carries a discrete
121	valuation `v_Y` on the function field `K(X)`. The order of a nonzero rational
122	function `f ∈ K(X)^×` along `Y` is the integer `ord_Y(f) := v_Y(f)`. -/
123	
124	namespace Scheme.RationalMap
125	
126	/-- **Order of a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×` along a prime divisor `Y`.**
127	
128	For `X` satisfying Hartshorne's `(*)`, the local ring `O_{X,η}` at the generic
129	point `η` of `Y` is a discrete valuation ring with quotient field the function
130	field `K(X)`, and `ord_Y(f) = v_Y(f) ∈ ℤ` is the value of the associated normalised
131	discrete valuation on `f`.
132	
133	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, every closed point `P ∈ C` is a prime
134	divisor and `ord_P(f) = v_P(f)` is the standard DVR valuation at `P`.
135	
136	Blueprint reference: `def:order_at_point` (Hartshorne II §6 pp. 130–131; Stacks 02ME).
137	
138	iter-176 body (per analogist `dvr-rationalmap-order`): the body uses Mathlib's
139	`Ring.ordFrac` (the canonical `K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` monoid-with-zero hom from
140	`Mathlib.RingTheory.OrderOfVanishing.Basic`, Stacks `02MD`) on the stalk
141	`X.presheaf.stalk Y.point`, then projects through `WithZero.log : ℤᵐ⁰ → ℤ`
142	(the canonical projection with junk-on-zero, `Mathlib.Algebra.GroupWithZero.WithZero`).
143	On `f = 0` this gives `order Y 0 = 0` (junk convention from `WithZero.log_zero`).
144	
145	The required Mathlib typeclasses on the stalk are:
146	- `IsNoetherianRing` — from `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]`.
147	- `IsDomain` ⟹ `Nontrivial` — from `[IsIntegral X]`.
148	- `IsFractionRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) X.functionField` — from `[IsIntegral X]`.
149	- `Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` — threaded explicitly (the
150	  topological-coheight-to-Krull-dim bridge `Order.coheight Y.point = 1 ⟹
151	  Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` is a Mathlib-upstream-pending
152	  gap; see Stacks `02IZ` / `005X`). -/
153	noncomputable def order {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
154	    [IsLocallyNoetherian X] (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)
155	    [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)]
156	    (f : X.functionField) : ℤ :=
157	  WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) f)
158	
159	end Scheme.RationalMap

161	/-! ### Regular-in-codimension-one bridge class
162	
163	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` includes "regular in codimension one", i.e.\ every
164	prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a DVR stalk `O_{X,Y.point}`. Mathlib has no direct
165	typeclass for this; we package it as the project-bespoke class
166	`Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne`, with an instance synthesising the per-`Y`
167	Krull-dim-≤-1 condition required by `Scheme.RationalMap.order`. Blueprint pin
168	("Iter-173+ may introduce a `Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne` predicate to
169	abbreviate this"; chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §2 "Standing hypothesis
170	`(*)` in the Lean encoding"). -/
171	
172	/-- Project-bespoke class encoding Hartshorne's "regular in codimension one"
173	clause of `(*)`: every prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a DVR stalk
174	`O_{X,Y.point}`. The `[IsIntegral X]` precondition makes
175	`IsDomain (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` available so that the
176	`IsDiscreteValuationRing` predicate is well-formed
177	(`AlgebraicGeometry.instIsDomainCarrierStalkCommRingCatPresheafOfIsIntegral`). -/
178	class Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne (X : Scheme.{u}) [IsIntegral X] : Prop where
179	  /-- The defining content: every prime divisor's stalk is a discrete valuation
180	  ring. (This is the precise content of Hartshorne's `(*)`; the weaker
181	  `Ring.KrullDimLE 1` derives via the `IsDiscreteValuationRing` ⟹
182	  `IsPrincipalIdealRing` ⟹ `Ring.KrullDimLE 1` chain via the bridge
183	  instance below.) -/
184	  out : ∀ Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X, IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)

187	synthesis can derive `IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` for

190	    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
191	    (Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X) :
192	    IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
193	  Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.out Y

196	synthesis can derive `Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` for every
197	prime divisor `Y`, via the `IsDiscreteValuationRing` ⟹ `IsPrincipalIdealRing`
198	⟹ `Ring.KrullDimLE 1` chain (Mathlib's `IsDiscreteValuationRing.toIsPrincipalIdealRing`
199	+ `IsPrincipalIdealRing.krullDimLE_one`). -/

201	    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]

207	    IsDiscreteValuationRing.toIsPrincipalIdealRing
208	  IsPrincipalIdealRing.krullDimLE_one _

210	/-- **Hartshorne II.6.1**: for a nonzero rational function `f` on a Noetherian
211	integral scheme `X` satisfying `(*)`, the order function `Y ↦ ord_Y(f)` is
212	nonzero at only finitely many prime divisors `Y`. This is the well-definedness
213	side condition for `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal`.

216	ship. The body is a Mathlib-upstream-pending gap (Stacks tag `02RV` — for a

225	`f = 0` the function `Y ↦ ord_Y(0) = WithZero.log 0 = 0` has empty support,
226	which is finite, so the conclusion holds vacuously. -/
227	private theorem rationalMap_order_finite_support {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
228	    [IsLocallyNoetherian X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
229	    (f : X.functionField) :
230	    (Function.support (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor =>
231	      Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)).Finite := by

234	  -- Case 1 (f = 0): the order function evaluates to
235	  -- `WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac _ 0) = WithZero.log 0 = 0` at every
236	  -- prime divisor, so the support is empty (finite vacuously).
237	  by_cases hf : f = 0
238	  · subst hf
239	    convert Set.finite_empty
240	    ext Y
241	    simp only [Function.mem_support, ne_eq, Set.mem_empty_iff_false, iff_false,
242	      Decidable.not_not, Scheme.RationalMap.order, map_zero, WithZero.log_zero]
243	  -- Case 2 (f ≠ 0): genuinely needs Hartshorne II.6.1 (Stacks 02RV).

245	  -- "for a Noetherian integral scheme, only finitely many height-1
246	  -- primes can divide the numerator or denominator of a nonzero rational

248	  -- Body owed iter-193+; iter-192 closes only the f = 0 vacuous case.

255	/-! ## §3. Divisor of a closed point on a curve
256	
257	On a smooth proper curve `C` over a field, every closed point `P ∈ C` is a prime
258	divisor (it is closed, integral, of codimension one in the one-dimensional integral
259	scheme `C`). The associated Weil divisor is `[P] = 1 · P ∈ Div(C)`. -/
260	
261	/-- **The Weil divisor associated to a closed point `P` on a curve.** The element
262	`[P] := 1 · P ∈ Div(C)`, i.e. the prime divisor `P` with multiplicity one.
263	
264	For a smooth proper curve every closed point is automatically a prime divisor (it
265	is a codimension-one integral closed subscheme of the one-dimensional integral
266	scheme `C`); conversely every prime divisor on a curve is of this form, so an
267	arbitrary divisor on `C` is a finite formal `ℤ`-linear combination
268	`Σ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]` of closed points.
269	
270	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_closed_point` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 137).
271	
272	iter-174 body: the function is junk-defined outside its intended scope. We
273	case-split on `Order.coheight P = 1` (the codimension-one witness of
274	`PrimeDivisor`). On the branch where the equality holds — automatic for a
275	closed point on a one-dimensional integral scheme — we promote `P` to a
276	`PrimeDivisor` via the witness and return `Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1`, i.e.
277	the prime divisor `P` with multiplicity one. On the off-branch (junk regime)
278	we return the zero divisor. The blueprint pins the well-definedness argument
279	"`IsClosed {P}` on a one-dimensional integral scheme ⟹ `coheight P = 1`" as a
280	separate threadable hypothesis at the call site (chapter L330–L340 "Lean
281	signature scope"); see `ofClosedPoint_eq_single` for the equation in the
282	hypothesised regime. -/
283	noncomputable def ofClosedPoint {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
284	    (_hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) : C.WeilDivisor :=
285	  if h : Order.coheight P = 1 then Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1 else 0
286	
287	/-- In the hypothesised regime where the closed point `P` has coheight one
288	(the codim-1 condition automatic for a closed point of a one-dimensional
289	integral scheme), `ofClosedPoint P hP` is the prime divisor `P` with
290	multiplicity one. -/
291	lemma ofClosedPoint_eq_single {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
292	    (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) (h : Order.coheight P = 1) :
293	    ofClosedPoint P hP = Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1 := by
294	  simp [ofClosedPoint, h]
295	
296	/-- Off-branch: outside the codim-1 regime, `ofClosedPoint` is junk-defined as
297	the zero divisor. (Only relevant when the user supplies a "closed point" that
298	does not have coheight one in the ambient scheme — e.g. a generic point or a
299	codim-≥2 point on a higher-dimensional scheme.) -/
300	lemma ofClosedPoint_eq_zero {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)

300	lemma ofClosedPoint_eq_zero {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
301	    (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) (h : Order.coheight P ≠ 1) :
302	    ofClosedPoint P hP = 0 := by
303	  simp [ofClosedPoint, h]

305	/-! ## §4. Degree of a divisor over an algebraically closed base -/
306	
307	/-- **Degree of a Weil divisor on a smooth proper curve over `k̄`.**
308	
309	Over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every closed point of a smooth proper
310	curve `C` has residue field `k̄`, so each prime divisor `[P]` contributes degree
311	one to the sum, and `deg(D) := Σᵢ nᵢ` is the sum of the integer coefficients of
312	the formal sum `D = Σ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]`.
313	
314	(Over a general field `k` one weights by the residue-field degrees
315	`Σᵢ nᵢ · [κ(Pᵢ) : k]` to recover the geometric degree; the project's RR bridge
316	needs only the `k̄` specialisation.)
317	
318	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_degree` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 137; Stacks 0BE0).
319	
320	Implementation: `Finsupp.sum D (fun _ n => n)` is the sum of all coefficients of
321	the finitely supported function representing `D`. -/
322	noncomputable def degree (D : X.WeilDivisor) : ℤ :=
323	  (D : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ).sum (fun _ n => n)
324	
325	/-- **The degree map is a group homomorphism `Div(C) → ℤ`.**
326	
327	`deg(D₁ + D₂) = deg(D₁) + deg(D₂)`, `deg(-D) = -deg(D)`, `deg(0) = 0`. Bundled
328	as an `AddMonoidHom` for downstream use (the linear-equivalence quotient
329	`Cl(C) := Div(C) / im(div)` will inherit a `deg : Cl(C) → ℤ` from this).
330	
331	Blueprint reference: `thm:divisor_degree_hom` (immediate from `def:divisor_degree`).
332	
333	Implementation: built from `Finsupp.liftAddHom (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ)`, the
334	generic Mathlib packaging that lifts a family of `AddMonoidHom`s indexed by the
335	support into a single `AddMonoidHom` on the finsupp. Unfolds to
336	`D.sum (fun _ z ↦ z) = degree D` (see `degree_hom_apply` below). -/
337	noncomputable def degree_hom : X.WeilDivisor →+ ℤ :=
338	  Finsupp.liftAddHom (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ)
339	
340	@[simp]
341	lemma degree_hom_apply (D : X.WeilDivisor) : degree_hom D = degree D :=
342	  Finsupp.liftAddHom_apply (α := X.PrimeDivisor) (M := ℤ) (N := ℤ)
343	    (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ) D
344	
345	/-- **Degree of the zero divisor is zero.** A direct consequence of
346	`degree_hom` being an `AddMonoidHom`. -/
347	@[simp]
348	lemma degree_zero : degree (0 : X.WeilDivisor) = 0 := by
349	  rw [← degree_hom_apply]; exact map_zero _
350	
351	/-- **Degree is additive.** A direct consequence of `degree_hom` being an
352	`AddMonoidHom`. -/
353	lemma degree_add (D₁ D₂ : X.WeilDivisor) :
354	    degree (D₁ + D₂) = degree D₁ + degree D₂ := by
355	  rw [← degree_hom_apply, ← degree_hom_apply, ← degree_hom_apply]
356	  exact map_add _ _ _
357	
358	/-! ## §5. Principal divisors -/
359	
360	/-- **The principal divisor of a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×`.**
361	
362	By Hartshorne's Lemma 6.1, `ord_Y(f) = 0` for all but finitely many prime
363	divisors `Y`, so the formal sum
364	`div(f) := Σ_{Y prime divisor} ord_Y(f) · Y ∈ Div(X)`
365	has finite support and is a well-defined Weil divisor.
366	
367	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, this specialises to
368	`div(f) = Σ_{P closed point} ord_P(f) · [P]`.
369	
370	Blueprint reference: `def:principal_divisor` (Hartshorne II §6 Lemma 6.1 +
371	following definition, p. 131).
372	
373	iter-177 body: the construction uses `Finsupp.ofSupportFinite` with the
374	finite-support witness `rationalMap_order_finite_support`. The latter is a
375	private theorem packaging Hartshorne 6.1; its body is a Mathlib-pending gap
376	(see chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §5 sub-build note) and is left as
377	a `sorry` for an iter-178+ Mathlib-upstream PR. -/
378	noncomputable def principal [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
379	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (f : X.functionField)
380	    (_hf : f ≠ 0) : X.WeilDivisor :=
381	  Finsupp.ofSupportFinite
382	    (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor => Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)
383	    (rationalMap_order_finite_support f)
384	
385	/-- **The coefficient of `principal f hf` at a prime divisor `Y` is the order of
386	`f` along `Y`.** This is the basic structural unfolding of the
387	`Finsupp.ofSupportFinite` packaging in `principal`; one-line via
388	`Finsupp.ofSupportFinite_coe` from `Mathlib.Data.Finsupp.Defs`.
389	
390	iter-193 substrate helper for the Lane I body close
391	(`degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`). -/
392	lemma principal_apply [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
393	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (f : X.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0)
394	    (Y : X.PrimeDivisor) :
395	    (show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf) Y =
396	      Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f := by
397	  change (Finsupp.ofSupportFinite
398	      (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor => Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)
399	      (rationalMap_order_finite_support f)) Y = _
400	  rw [Finsupp.ofSupportFinite_coe]
401	
402	/-- **`Scheme.RationalMap.order Y 1 = 0`** — the order of the constant
403	function `1` is `0` at every prime divisor. Direct from
404	`map_one` of `Ring.ordFrac` + `WithZero.log_one`.
405	
406	iter-193 substrate helper for further structural results
407	(`principal_one_eq_zero` and similar). -/
408	@[simp]
409	lemma _root_.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.order_one
410	    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
411	    (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)
412	    [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)] :
413	    Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (1 : X.functionField) = 0 := by
414	  unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
415	  rw [map_one, WithZero.log_one]
416	
417	/-- **The principal divisor of the constant function `1` is the zero
418	divisor.** A direct consequence of `Scheme.RationalMap.order_one`. -/
419	@[simp]
420	lemma principal_one [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
421	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] :
422	    principal (1 : X.functionField) one_ne_zero = 0 := by
423	  change (_ : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) = (0 : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ)
424	  apply Finsupp.ext
425	  intro Y
426	  rw [principal_apply]
427	  exact Scheme.RationalMap.order_one Y
428	
429	/-- **The principal-divisor map is a group homomorphism `K(X)^× → Div(X)`.**
430	
431	Concretely `div(fg) = div(f) + div(g)`, `div(f⁻¹) = -div(f)`, `div(1) = 0`. Bundled
432	as a `MonoidHom` from the multiplicative units of `K(X)` to `Multiplicative (Div(X))`
433	(equivalently: an additive map `(K(X)^×, ·) → (Div(X), +)`).
434	
435	Blueprint reference: `thm:principal_hom` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 131).
436	
437	iter-177 body: closes coordinate-wise from the DVR identities
438	`v_Y(fg) = v_Y(f) + v_Y(g)`, `v_Y(1) = 0`. The per-`Y` identities live in
439	`Scheme.RationalMap.order` via `Ring.ordFrac` (a `K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` monoid-with-zero
440	hom) and `WithZero.log_mul` / `WithZero.log_one`. -/
441	noncomputable def principal_hom [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
442	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] :
443	    (X.functionField)ˣ →* Multiplicative X.WeilDivisor where
444	  toFun u :=
445	    Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal (↑u : X.functionField) (Units.ne_zero u))
446	  map_one' := by
447	    -- Goal: Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑1 _) = 1.
448	    -- Use `← ofAdd_zero` to rewrite RHS as `Multiplicative.ofAdd 0`,
449	    -- then `congr 1` reduces to `principal ↑1 _ = 0` (Finsupp equality),
450	    -- and `Finsupp.ext` peels to the per-`Y` coordinate identity, which
451	    -- closes by unfolding `order` to `WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac _ 1) = 0`
452	    -- via `map_one` and `WithZero.log_one`.
453	    rw [← ofAdd_zero]
454	    congr 1
455	    apply Finsupp.ext
456	    intro Y
457	    change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y
458	        (((1 : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField)) = 0
459	    rw [Units.val_one]
460	    unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
461	    rw [map_one, WithZero.log_one]
462	  map_mul' u v := by
463	    -- Goal: Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑(u*v) _) =
464	    --       Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑u _) * Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑v _).
465	    -- Use `← ofAdd_add` to rewrite RHS, then `congr 1` reduces to
466	    -- `principal ↑(u*v) _ = principal ↑u _ + principal ↑v _` (Finsupp
467	    -- equality), and `Finsupp.ext` peels to the per-`Y` coordinate
468	    -- identity `order Y (uv) = order Y u + order Y v`, which closes by
469	    -- `Ring.ordFrac (uv) = Ring.ordFrac u * Ring.ordFrac v` (`map_mul`)
470	    -- and `WithZero.log_mul` (the nonzero hypotheses come from
471	    -- `Units.ne_zero` and `map_ne_zero`).
472	    rw [← ofAdd_add]
473	    congr 1
474	    apply Finsupp.ext
475	    intro Y
476	    change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y ((↑(u * v) : X.functionField))
477	      = Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (↑u : X.functionField)
478	      + Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (↑v : X.functionField)
479	    rw [Units.val_mul]
480	    unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
481	    rw [map_mul]
482	    exact WithZero.log_mul
483	      ((map_ne_zero _).mpr (Units.ne_zero u))
484	      ((map_ne_zero _).mpr (Units.ne_zero v))
485	
486	/-- **Principal divisors on a complete nonsingular curve have degree zero**
487	(Hartshorne Corollary II.6.10, Stacks 0BE3).
488	
489	For every nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(C)^×` on a smooth proper curve `C`
490	over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
491	`deg(div(f)) = 0 ∈ ℤ`.
492	
493	Blueprint reference: `thm:principal_deg_zero` (Hartshorne II.6 Cor. 6.10 p. 138).
494	
495	The proof (Hartshorne 6.10): if `f ∈ k̄` is constant then `div(f) = 0` and the
496	claim is trivial. Otherwise the inclusion `k̄(f) ⊂ K(C)` exhibits `K(C)` as a
497	finite extension of `k̄(f) ≅ k̄(t)`, so the corresponding morphism
498	`φ : C → ℙ¹_{k̄}` is finite, `div(f) = φ^*([0] - [∞])`, and pullback along a
499	finite morphism multiplies degree by `deg(φ)`. Two auxiliary sub-lemmas
500	(finite morphism induced by a non-constant rational function; multiplicativity
501	of degree under finite pullback, Hartshorne II.6.9) are deferred to follow-up
502	iters of `RR.1`. -/
503	theorem principal_degree_zero {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
504	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
505	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
506	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
507	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
508	    (f : C.left.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0) :
509	    degree (principal f hf) = 0 := by
510	  -- Hartshorne II.6.10 (iter-178 partial). Case-split on whether every
511	  -- prime-divisor order of `f` vanishes. On a complete nonsingular curve,
512	  -- this case split exactly recovers Hartshorne's constant-vs-non-constant
513	  -- split: `(∀ Y, ord_Y f = 0)` ⟺ `f ∈ k̄ \ {0}` (a nowhere-vanishing
514	  -- rational function on a complete curve is constant; that direction is
515	  -- Hartshorne II.6.10's "if `f ∈ k̄` then `(f) = 0`").
516	  by_cases hconst : ∀ Y : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f = 0
517	  · -- Constant branch: every order vanishes ⟹ `principal f hf = 0` ⟹
518	    -- `degree (principal f hf) = degree 0 = 0`.
519	    have hprincipal_zero : principal f hf = 0 := by
520	      apply Finsupp.ext
521	      intro Y
522	      change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f = 0
523	      exact hconst Y
524	    rw [hprincipal_zero]
525	    unfold degree
526	    exact Finsupp.sum_zero_index
527	  · -- Non-constant branch (Hartshorne II.6.10 `f ∉ k̄`): the inclusion
528	    -- `k̄(f) ⊂ K(C)` defines a finite morphism `φ : C → ℙ¹_{k̄}` via the
529	    -- function-field-determines-curve correspondence (Hartshorne I.6.12),
530	    -- and `principal f hf = φ^*([0] - [∞])`. Pullback along a finite
531	    -- morphism of curves multiplies degrees by `deg(φ)` (Hartshorne
532	    -- II.6.9), so `deg(principal f hf) = deg(φ) · deg([0] - [∞]) =
533	    -- deg(φ) · 0 = 0`.
534	    -- This branch is gated on (i) the `φ : C → ℙ¹` construction (Lane 5
535	    -- `RationalCurveIso.lean:morphismToP1OfGlobalSections`, iter-178+),
536	    -- and (ii) the degree-multiplicativity-under-finite-pullback bridge
537	    -- (Hartshorne II.6.9, Mathlib gap).
538	    sorry
