540	/-! ## §6. Positive part of a Weil divisor
541	
542	Iter-190 plan-phase Lane I Pin 2 corrective substrate. The carrier
543	`X.WeilDivisor = X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ` is a finitely supported integer-valued
544	function on prime divisors; the *positive part* `(D)_0` is obtained by
545	replacing each coefficient `n_Y` with `max(n_Y, 0)`. Equivalently this is
546	the lattice join `D ⊔ 0` in the pointwise lattice structure (the
547	`semilatticeSup` instance on `Finsupp` is noncomputable). The complementary
548	*negative part* `(D)_∞ := (-D)_0` then gives the canonical decomposition
549	`D = (D)_0 - (D)_∞` into a difference of effective divisors.
550	
551	Blueprint reference: `def:WeilDivisor_positivePart` /
552	`lem:degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` of
553	`RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §6 (iter-190 plan-phase additions).
554	-/
555	
556	/-- **Positive part of a Weil divisor**.
557	
558	`positivePart D` is the divisor obtained from `D` by replacing each
559	coefficient `n_Y` with `max(n_Y, 0)` (equivalently `D ⊔ 0` in the
560	pointwise lattice structure on `Finsupp`). For `D = div(f)` on a smooth
561	proper curve `C`, this recovers the divisor-of-zeros `(f)_0` — the
562	formal sum of the zeros of `f` with their multiplicities.
563	
564	The complementary `negativePart D := positivePart (-D)` then gives
565	`D = positivePart D - negativePart D` as a difference of effective
566	divisors.
567	
568	Implementation: `Finsupp.mapRange (fun n : ℤ => n ⊔ 0) (by simp)`, the
569	generic Mathlib packaging that maps a finsupp's coefficients through a
570	zero-preserving function. (The `D ⊔ 0` lattice form is equivalent but
571	requires the noncomputable `Finsupp.semilatticeSup` synthesis on
572	`PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ`; the explicit `mapRange` form is more transparent
573	to typeclass synthesis.)
574	
575	Blueprint reference: `def:WeilDivisor_positivePart` (project-bespoke;
576	chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §6, Hartshorne II.6.10 phrasing). -/
577	noncomputable def positivePart (D : X.WeilDivisor) : X.WeilDivisor :=
578	  Finsupp.mapRange (fun n : ℤ => n ⊔ 0) (by simp) D
579	
580	/-- The positive part of the zero divisor is zero. -/
581	@[simp]
582	lemma positivePart_zero : positivePart (0 : X.WeilDivisor) = 0 := by
583	  change Finsupp.mapRange (fun n : ℤ => n ⊔ 0) (by simp)
584	    (0 : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) = (0 : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ)
585	  exact Finsupp.mapRange_zero
586	
587	/-- **Degree of positive part as a sum of capped coefficients.** A purely
588	symbolic Mathlib manipulation: unfolding `positivePart`
589	(= `Finsupp.mapRange (max · 0)`) and `degree` (= `Finsupp.sum (fun _ n => n)`)
590	identifies `degree (positivePart D)` with the sum of `max (D Y) 0` over
591	the support of `D`.
592	
593	This is the iter-192 structural-reduction helper consumed by
594	`degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` below. The proof is one line
595	via `Finsupp.sum_mapRange_index` from
596	`Mathlib.Algebra.BigOperators.Finsupp.Basic`. -/
597	lemma degree_positivePart_eq_sum_max (D : X.WeilDivisor) :
598	    degree (positivePart D) = D.sum (fun _ n => max n 0) := by
599	  unfold positivePart degree
600	  exact Finsupp.sum_mapRange_index (h := fun _ b => b) (by intro _; rfl)
601	
602	/-- **Positive part of a `Finsupp.single`.** Pointwise extraction of the
603	mapRange definition of `positivePart`: for a one-point-supported Weil divisor
604	`Finsupp.single Y n`, the positive part is `Finsupp.single Y (max n 0)`.
605	A direct consequence of `Finsupp.mapRange_single` (with `max 0 0 = 0` as
606	the zero-preservation witness).
607	
608	iter-193 substrate helper paving the way for the Lane I body close. -/
609	@[simp]
610	lemma positivePart_single (Y : X.PrimeDivisor) (n : ℤ) :
611	    positivePart (Finsupp.single Y n : X.WeilDivisor) =
612	      Finsupp.single Y (max n 0) := by
613	  change Finsupp.mapRange (fun n : ℤ => n ⊔ 0) (by simp)
614	      (Finsupp.single Y n) = _
615	  rw [Finsupp.mapRange_single]
616	
617	/-- **Degree of a `Finsupp.single` Weil divisor.** The degree of the
618	one-point-supported Weil divisor `Finsupp.single Y n` is `n`. A direct
619	consequence of `Finsupp.sum_single_index`.
620	
621	iter-193 substrate helper for `degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`. -/
622	@[simp]
623	lemma degree_single (Y : X.PrimeDivisor) (n : ℤ) :
624	    degree (Finsupp.single Y n : X.WeilDivisor) = n := by
625	  unfold degree
626	  change (Finsupp.single Y n : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ).sum (fun _ n => n) = n
627	  exact Finsupp.sum_single_index rfl
628	
629	/-- **Sum-over-prime-divisors lower bound via a single contributing point.**
630	If `f` has order `1` at some prime divisor `Y₀`, then the degree of the positive
631	part of `principal f hf` is at least `1`.
632	
633	iter-193 substrate helper consumed by `degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`:
634	it formalises Step 2 of the Hartshorne II.6.9 recipe (`hlp` produces
635	the local-parameter prime divisor `Y₀` with coefficient `1`). -/
636	lemma one_le_degree_positivePart_principal_of_order_one
637	    [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
638	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
639	    (f : X.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0) (Y₀ : X.PrimeDivisor)
640	    (h₀ : Scheme.RationalMap.order Y₀ f = 1) :
641	    1 ≤ degree (positivePart (principal f hf)) := by
642	  classical
643	  rw [degree_positivePart_eq_sum_max]
644	  -- The sum is over a finset; isolate the Y₀ contribution.
645	  have hY₀_supp :
646	      Y₀ ∈ (show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf).support := by
647	    rw [Finsupp.mem_support_iff]
648	    rw [principal_apply]
649	    rw [h₀]

370	`ConnectedSpace (pullback f g)` instance for
371	`[GeometricallyConnected f] [UniversallyOpen f] [ConnectedSpace Y]`
372	to give the key "`G⁰ ×_k G⁰` is connected" substrate for Kleiman's
373	group-structure inheritance argument in `isSubgroupHomomorphism`. -/
374	private instance identityComponent_connectedSpace
375	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
376	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
377	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
378	    ConnectedSpace (IdentityComponent G).left := by
379	  change ConnectedSpace ((identityComponentCarrier G : G.left.Opens) : Type _)
380	  infer_instance
381	
382	/-- **Stacks 04KU / EGA IV₂ 4.5.14**: a connected `k`-scheme with a `k`-rational
383	section is geometrically connected.
384	
385	Given a morphism `f : X ⟶ Spec k` from a `ConnectedSpace`-typed scheme `X`
386	admitting a section `s : Spec k ⟶ X` (i.e. `s ≫ f = 𝟙`), the morphism `f` is
387	geometrically connected: for any field extension `K/k`, the pullback
388	`X ×_{Spec k} Spec K` is connected.
389	
390	iter-193 Lane A.3.i, planner-set authorisation per `PROGRESS.md`: ships as a
391	typed `sorry` to enable downstream consumers (sanctioned temporary sorry-count
392	increase). The full body is Stacks 04KV: a hypothetical clopen partition
393	`X_K = U ⊔ V` would, via the base-changed section `s_K = s ≫ (pullback π s)⁻¹`
394	say `K`-rational point landing in exactly one of `U, V`; the image of the
395	chosen piece under the projection `X_K → X` would then be a clopen subset
396	of `X` whose disjointness from the image of the other piece contradicts
397	`ConnectedSpace X`. Mathlib at SHA b80f227 lacks the descent-of-clopen-
398	partitions-along-fpqc-base-change substrate (Stacks 02LB), preventing an
399	axiom-clean closure this iter.
400	
401	Alternative proof via Stacks 037Q (also project-side, also not yet built):
402	a `k`-scheme `X` is geometrically connected iff `X` is connected AND the
403	algebraic closure of `k` in `Γ(X, 𝒪_X)` equals `k`. With the section `s`,
404	the pullback `s^* : Γ(X, 𝒪_X) → k` is a `k`-algebra retraction; any subfield
405	`F ⊆ Γ(X, 𝒪_X)` containing `k` maps injectively into `k` (as `F` is a field),
406	forcing `F = k`. Combined with `ConnectedSpace X`, gives the conclusion.
407	
408	Downstream consumers:
409	- iso slot of `IdentityComponent.baseChangeIso` (the `(G^0)_K ≅ (G_K)^0`
410	  identification);
411	- chain into `[GeometricallyConnected f] [UniversallyOpen f]
412	  [ConnectedSpace Y] ⟹ ConnectedSpace (pullback f g)` to derive
413	  `ConnectedSpace (G^0 ×_k G^0)` for `isSubgroupHomomorphism`. -/
414	private theorem geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section
415	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {X : Scheme.{u}}
416	    (f : X ⟶ Spec (.of k))
417	    (s : Spec (.of k) ⟶ X) (_hsf : s ≫ f = 𝟙 _)
418	    [ConnectedSpace X] :
419	    GeometricallyConnected f := by
420	  -- iter-194 push-beyond restructure: expose the structural reduction via
421	  -- `geometrically_iff_of_commRing_of_isClosedUnderIsomorphisms`. The
422	  -- residual sorry now lives at the precise Stacks 037Q gap point.
423	  refine ⟨?_⟩
424	  rw [geometrically_iff_of_commRing_of_isClosedUnderIsomorphisms]
425	  intro K _ _
426	  -- Goal: `ConnectedSpace ↥(pullback f (Spec.map (ofHom (algebraMap k K))))`.
427	  --
428	  -- Stacks 037Q gap (precise INCOMPLETE surface, iter-194 prover lane):
429	  -- The Stacks-037Q route requires two missing Mathlib pieces at SHA b80f227:
430	  --
431	  -- (1) **Stacks 04KV reduction**: For `f : X → Spec k`, `GeometricallyConnected f`
432	  --     iff for every finite separable extension `k'/k`, the base change
433	  --     `X ×_k Spec k'` is connected. This is the descent of geometric
434	  --     connectedness from arbitrary extensions to finite separable ones; it
435	  --     uses Galois-fixed-point arguments on `Spec(k' ⊗_k K)` for `K`
436	  --     algebraically closed. Not in Mathlib.
437	  --
438	  -- (2) **Field-tensor-product criterion**: For `K = Γ(X, O_X)` with `k`
439	  --     algebraically closed in `K`, the tensor product `K ⊗_k k'` is a
440	  --     field for every finite separable extension `k'/k`. Not in Mathlib
441	  --     (the converse `Algebra.TensorProduct.isField_of_isAlgebraic` exists
442	  --     but expects `IsDomain` on the tensor product; the "alg-closed-in"
443	  --     hypothesis is not what `Algebra.IsAlgebraic` packages).
444	  --
445	  -- The section `s` supplies the algebraic-closure hypothesis of (2): the
446	  -- `k`-algebra retraction `s.app top : Γ(X, O_X) → k` of `f.app top`
447	  -- forces any intermediate subfield `F` of `Γ(X, O_X)` containing `k` to
448	  -- satisfy `F = k` (a `k`-algebra retraction of a field map is necessarily
449	  -- an isomorphism). With (2) in hand, `Spec(Γ(X, O_X) ⊗_k k')` is connected
450	  -- (Spec of a field is connected), then (1) chains this to the conclusion.
451	  --
452	  -- Alternative attack: descent of clopen partitions along faithfully flat
453	  -- (Spec K → Spec k) base change. Mathlib has
454	  -- `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.isQuotientMap_of_surjective` for the
455	  -- faithfully-flat-quasi-compact-surjective case; the fibre-connectedness
456	  -- needed for the quotient-map argument to bootstrap is the same gap.
457	  --
458	  -- iter-200 mathlib-analogist sweep target: surface (1) + (2) on the
459	  -- "substrate unowned" tier; the project-side closure becomes mechanical
460	  -- once either is in Mathlib.
461	  --
462	  -- iter-194 structural advance (axiom-clean intermediate): build the
463	  -- base-changed section `sK` explicitly so the next prover starts from
464	  -- "ConnectedSpace + Nonempty witness" instead of bare connectedness.
465	  -- This is what the `_hsf` hypothesis encodes structurally — the
466	  -- `s ≫ f = 𝟙` triangle base-changes to a section of `pullback.snd`,
467	  -- giving a `K`-rational point of the base change.
468	  let g : Spec (CommRingCat.of K) ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k) :=
469	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap k K))
470	  let _sK : Spec (CommRingCat.of K) ⟶ Limits.pullback f g :=
471	    Limits.pullback.lift (g ≫ s) (𝟙 _)
472	      (by rw [Category.assoc, _hsf, Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp])
473	  -- `_sK` is a section of `pullback.snd : pullback f g → Spec K` (by
474	  -- `pullback.lift_snd`); pushing `default` through gives nonemptiness:
475	  haveI : Nonempty ↑↑(Limits.pullback f g).toPresheafedSpace :=
476	    ⟨_sK.base default⟩
477	  -- Residual gap: connectedness of `pullback f g` given nonemptiness +
478	  -- `ConnectedSpace X`. Stacks 037Q / 04KV; cf. structural notes above.
479	  sorry
480	
481	/-- The range-containment hypothesis for `IsOpenImmersion.lift` used to build
482	the section `identityComponentSection G` below: the image of the identity
483	section `MonObj.one.left : Spec k ⟶ G.left` lies in the carrier of the
484	identity component (a singleton image `{identitySectionPoint G}` contained
485	in `connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G) = identityComponentCarrier G`). -/
486	private lemma identityComponentSection_range_subset
487	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
488	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
489	    Set.range ((MonObj.one (X := G)).left : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left).base ⊆
490	      Set.range (identityComponentCarrier G).ι.base := by
491	  rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
492	  let f : ↥(Spec (.of k)) → G.left := (MonObj.one (X := G)).left.base
493	  change Set.range f ⊆ _
494	  rintro y ⟨x, rfl⟩
495	  have hx : x = default := Subsingleton.elim _ _
496	  rw [hx]
497	  -- `f default = identitySectionPoint G` (by definition of
498	  -- `identitySectionPoint`); the carrier underlying set is
499	  -- `connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G)`.
500	  exact mem_connectedComponent
501	
502	private noncomputable def identityComponentSection
503	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
504	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
505	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
506	    Spec (.of k) ⟶ (IdentityComponent G).left :=
507	  @IsOpenImmersion.lift (identityComponentCarrier G).toScheme (Spec (.of k))
508	    G.left (identityComponentCarrier G).ι
509	    ((MonObj.one (X := G)).left : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left)
510	    inferInstance (identityComponentSection_range_subset G)
511	
512	/-- Helper (iter-193 Lane A.3.i, axiom-clean): the **identity section lift is
513	a section of `(IdentityComponent G).hom`**.
514	
515	Composing `identityComponentSection G` with `(IdentityComponent G).hom`
516	returns the identity on `Spec k`, by `IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac` plus the
517	over-compatibility `MonObj.one.left ≫ G.hom = 𝟙` (the terminal of
518	`Over (Spec k)` has `.hom = 𝟙 (Spec k)` definitionally). -/
519	private lemma identityComponentSection_isSection
520	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
521	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
522	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
523	    identityComponentSection G ≫ (IdentityComponent G).hom = 𝟙 (Spec (.of k)) := by
524	  -- `(IdentityComponent G).hom = (identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom`
525	  -- (definitionally, by the `IdentityComponent` def).
526	  change identityComponentSection G ≫ (identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom = _
527	  rw [← Category.assoc]
528	  -- `identityComponentSection G ≫ (identityComponentCarrier G).ι = MonObj.one.left`
529	  -- by `IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac` (after unfolding the `identityComponentSection`
530	  -- def to expose the `IsOpenImmersion.lift` head).
531	  rw [show identityComponentSection G ≫ (identityComponentCarrier G).ι =
532	        ((MonObj.one (X := G)).left : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left) from
533	      IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac _ _ (identityComponentSection_range_subset G)]
534	  -- Now: `(MonObj.one (X := G)).left ≫ G.hom = 𝟙 (Spec (.of k))`.
535	  -- This is the over-morphism compatibility: for the terminal of
536	  -- `Over (Spec k)`, `(MonObj.one).w` says
537	  -- `MonObj.one.left ≫ G.hom = (𝟙_).hom = 𝟙 (Spec k)` (defeq).
538	  exact (MonObj.one (X := G)).w
539	
540	/-- Lemma (iter-193 Lane A.3.i, propagates through the sorry-bodied
541	`geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section` helper): the structural
542	morphism `(IdentityComponent G).hom` is **geometrically connected**.
543	
544	**iter-194 demotion (per lean-auditor iter-193 must-fix): no longer a
545	`private instance` but a `private theorem`.** The earlier instance shape
546	silently propagated a `sorryAx` axiom into every downstream typeclass
547	search that happened to resolve `GeometricallyConnected (IdentityComponent
548	G).hom` — a soundness exposure. Downstream consumers should now invoke
549	this lemma explicitly via `letI := identityComponent_geometricallyConnected G`.
550	
551	Derived from:
552	- `identityComponent_connectedSpace`: `ConnectedSpace (IdentityComponent G).left`
553	  (axiom-clean iter-192).
554	- `identityComponentSection_isSection`: existence of a section
555	  `Spec k ⟶ (IdentityComponent G).left` (axiom-clean iter-193).
556	- `geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section`: Stacks 04KU helper
557	  (sorry-bodied iter-193, planner-sanctioned).
558	
559	Once `geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section` lands axiom-clean
560	(via the Stacks 037Q substrate or descent-of-clopen-partitions substrate),
561	this lemma becomes axiom-clean automatically. Downstream consumers can
562	chain `letI := identityComponent_geometricallyConnected G` with
563	`UniversallyOpen` of `Spec k → Spec k` (which holds via
564	`[IsIntegral Y] [Subsingleton Y] ⟹ UniversallyOpen f`) to derive
565	`ConnectedSpace (pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom g)` for any
566	`g : Y ⟶ Spec (.of k)` from a `ConnectedSpace Y`. -/
567	private theorem identityComponent_geometricallyConnected
568	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
569	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))

570	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
571	    GeometricallyConnected (IdentityComponent G).hom :=
572	  geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section
573	    (IdentityComponent G).hom (identityComponentSection G)
574	    (identityComponentSection_isSection G)
575	
576	/-- **The identity component inclusion is a group-scheme homomorphism.**
577	
578	Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3) conclusion (b): the clopen subscheme `G^0`
579	inherits a `k`-group-scheme structure from `G`, and the inclusion morphism
580	`G^0 ↪ G` (from `IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`) is compatible
581	with the group laws on source and target. The statement-level pin asserts
582	the existence of the inherited `GrpObj` structure; the compatibility of
583	the inclusion with the group operations is the substantive content of
584	Kleiman's argument (the product `G^0 ×_k G^0` is connected by
585	EGA IV₂ 4.5.8; the group-multiplication map sends this connected subset
586	containing the identity into the connected component `G^0`).
587	
588	The full statement (existence of `GrpObj (IdentityComponent G)` *together*
589	with the homomorphism-compatibility of the inclusion in the relevant
590	`Hom`-form) lives in iter-193+. -/
591	theorem IdentityComponent.isSubgroupHomomorphism {k : Type u} [Field k]
592	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
593	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
594	    Nonempty (GrpObj (IdentityComponent G)) :=
595	  sorry
596	
597	/-- **The identity component is of finite type and geometrically irreducible.**
598	
599	Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3) conclusion (c): the open subgroup `G^0` of
600	a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of finite type is itself
601	locally-of-finite-type-plus-quasi-compact (i.e., of finite type) over `k`,
602	and is geometrically irreducible. The proof reduces (after base change to
603	`\bar k`) to picking a nonempty smooth affine open subset `U ⊆ G^0`; the
604	translates `hg⁻¹U` give an open cover of `G^0` by smooth, hence irreducible,
605	neighbourhoods, so `G^0` is locally irreducible at every closed point; with
606	connectedness this gives irreducibility globally (EGA I 6.1.10). The image
607	`α(U × U) = G^0` is the image of the affine, hence quasi-compact, scheme
608	`U × U`, so `G^0` is quasi-compact.
609	
610	iter-189 partial progress: the `LocallyOfFiniteType` conjunct closes
611	axiom-clean via composition of an open immersion with `G.hom`. The
612	remaining two conjuncts (`QuasiCompact`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`)
613	require the group-structure argument and are bundled into the residual
614	sorry. -/
615	theorem IdentityComponent.isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible
616	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
617	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
618	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
619	    LocallyOfFiniteType (IdentityComponent G).hom ∧
620	      QuasiCompact (IdentityComponent G).hom ∧
621	      GeometricallyIrreducible (IdentityComponent G).hom := by
622	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
623	  · -- `LocallyOfFiniteType`: `(IdentityComponent G).hom` unfolds to
624	    -- `(identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom`. Open immersion ∘ LFT
625	    -- is LFT (`locallyOfFiniteType_of_isOpenImmersion` +
626	    -- `locallyOfFiniteType_comp`).
627	    change LocallyOfFiniteType ((identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom)
628	    infer_instance
629	  · -- `QuasiCompact ∧ GeometricallyIrreducible`: Kleiman's group-theoretic
630	    -- argument. `α(U × U) = G^0` quasi-compact via the affine `U × U`;
631	    -- geometric irreducibility via base change to `\bar k`, picking a
632	    -- smooth affine `U ⊆ G^0`, and translating `hg⁻¹U` to cover. Cannot
633	    -- close axiom-clean without the substrate `isSubgroupHomomorphism`
634	    -- + EGA IV₂ 4.5.8 / 4.6.1 / EGA I 6.1.10 (not yet in Mathlib).
635	    sorry
636	
637	/-- **Formation of the identity component commutes with base change.**
638	
639	Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3) conclusion (d): for any field extension
640	`K/k`, the natural comparison map `(G^0)_K → (G_K)^0` is an isomorphism of
641	`K`-schemes. The proof: `G^0` is geometrically connected as a connected
642	scheme with a `k`-rational point (EGA IV₂ 4.5.14), so its base change
643	`(G^0)_K` remains connected; it is also open and closed in `G_K`, so
644	coincides with the identity component `(G_K)^0`.
645	
646	The statement-level pin asserts existence of: a `K`-group-scheme structure
647	on the base change `G ×_{Spec k} Spec K` (with appropriate
648	locally-of-finite-type instance), and an isomorphism on underlying schemes
649	identifying the two iterated constructions.
650	
651	iter-192 partial closure (Lane A.3.i, axiom-clean modulo the iso slot):
652	the `_grpInst` slot closes via `CategoryTheory.Over.grpObjMkPullbackSnd`
653	(per `analogies/lane-a3i-isconnected-prod.md` second analogue — the
654	category-theoretic base-change of `GrpObj` directly fitting the
655	`Over.mk (pullback.snd ...)` shape of `G_K`); the `_locFTInst` slot
656	closes via Mathlib's stability of `LocallyOfFiniteType` under base
657	change (`inferInstance` fires on the pullback). The remaining iso slot
658	`(IdentityComponent G_K).left ≅ pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ`
659	is the substantive content of Stacks 04KS / EGA IV₂ 4.5.16: identifying
660	the iterated identity-component construction with the base change of
661	the identity component, which requires the
662	`geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section` helper above plus
663	descent of clopen partitions along base change (not yet in Mathlib). -/
664	theorem IdentityComponent.baseChangeIso {k : Type u} [Field k]
665	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
666	    (K : Type u) [Field K] [Algebra k K] :
667	    let φ : Spec (CommRingCat.of K) ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k) :=
668	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap k K))
669	    let G_K : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of K)) :=
670	      Over.mk (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.snd G.hom φ)
671	    Nonempty (Σ' (_grpInst : GrpObj G_K)
672	                 (_locFTInst : LocallyOfFiniteType G_K.hom),
673	      (IdentityComponent G_K).left ≅
674	        CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ) := by
675	  intro φ G_K
676	  -- `GrpObj G_K` via the category-theoretic base-change lemma
677	  -- `CategoryTheory.Over.grpObjMkPullbackSnd`. The instance bridge
678	  -- `[GrpObj G] ⟹ [GrpObj (Over.mk G.hom)]` is by defeq (`G = Over.mk G.hom`
679	  -- when `G` is an over-category object whose right component is the unique
680	  -- inhabitant of `Discrete PUnit`).
681	  haveI hG : GrpObj (Over.mk G.hom) := ‹GrpObj G›
682	  haveI hGK_grp : GrpObj G_K := CategoryTheory.Over.grpObjMkPullbackSnd
683	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType G_K.hom`: `G_K.hom = pullback.snd G.hom φ` is the
684	  -- base change of `G.hom` along `φ`; `LocallyOfFiniteType` is stable
685	  -- under base change (Mathlib instance).
686	  haveI hGK_lft : LocallyOfFiniteType G_K.hom :=
687	    (inferInstance : LocallyOfFiniteType (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.snd G.hom φ))
688	  -- The iso slot: substrate is now in place (iter-193 push-beyond):
689	  -- `identityComponent_geometricallyConnected` provides
690	  -- `GeometricallyConnected (IdentityComponent G).hom`, which combined with
691	  -- Mathlib's `[GeometricallyConnected f] [UniversallyOpen f]
692	  -- [ConnectedSpace Y] ⟹ ConnectedSpace (pullback f g)` instance gives
693	  -- `ConnectedSpace (pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ)` (the connected-
694	  -- pullback substrate). The iso construction proceeds by:
695	  -- 1. Build an open immersion `e : pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ ⟶
696	  --    G_K.left` via `pullback.map _ _ _ _ (identityComponentCarrier G).ι
697	  --    (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _) ...`, open-immersion status from
698	  --    `Scheme.pullback_map_isOpenImmersion` (i₁ = ι OI; i₂ = 𝟙 OI;
699	  --    i₃ = 𝟙 mono).
700	  -- 2. Show `Set.range e.base = identityComponentCarrier G_K (as Set)`
701	  --    via clopen-+-connected → connected-component bidirectional argument.
702	  -- 3. Apply `IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq` to get the iso between
703	  --    `pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ` and
704	  --    `(identityComponentCarrier G_K).toScheme = (IdentityComponent G_K).left`.
705	  -- Substantive content of step 2 (`~80-100 LOC`) deferred to iter-194+.
706	  refine ⟨⟨hGK_grp, hGK_lft, ?_⟩⟩
707	  sorry
708	
709	end GroupScheme
710	
711	/-! ## §2. The identity component of the Picard scheme
712	
713	We specialise the abstract identity-component substrate to
714	`G = PicScheme C`, the Picard scheme of a smooth proper geometrically
715	integral curve `C/k` (from sibling `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`).
716	
717	Blueprint reference: `def:pic_zero_subscheme` (Kleiman §5 opening + Prp.
718	`prp:pic0`). -/
719	
720	namespace Scheme
721	
722	/-- The **identity component of the Picard scheme** `Pic⁰_{C/k}`.
723	
724	Defined as the identity component
725	`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` of the Picard scheme
726	`Pic_{C/k}` (from sibling `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`,
727	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme`). By
728	`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`, `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an
729	open and closed subgroup scheme of `Pic_{C/k}` of finite type over `k`,
730	geometrically irreducible, and its formation commutes with extension of the
731	base field.
732	
733	iter-186+: the body unwinds to `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)`
734	once the `[LocallyOfFiniteType (PicScheme C).hom]` instance lands on
735	`PicScheme C` (it follows from the Kleiman §4 structure: `Pic_{C/k}` is
736	locally of finite type as the disjoint union of open quasi-projective
737	`k`-subschemes). For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
738	noncomputable def Pic0Scheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
739	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
740	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
741	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
742	    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
743	  sorry
744	
745	/-! ## §3. The degree map
746	
747	The disjoint-union structure of `Pic_{C/k}` (a disjoint union of open
748	quasi-projective `k`-subschemes, indexed by Hilbert polynomial via
749	`PicScheme.smoothProperQuotient`) stratifies its `T`-points by the leading
750	coefficient of the Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf
751	relative to a fixed degree-one polarisation. On `k`-points this gives the
752	**degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.
753	
754	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_degree_pic` (Milne III.1, p.~88). -/
755	
756	namespace PicScheme
757	
758	/-- The **degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.
759	
760	Sends a `k`-point `λ ∈ Pic_{C/k}(k)` --- a morphism
761	`Spec k ⟶ (PicScheme C).left` --- to the leading coefficient of the
762	Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf `L` on `C` (relative
763	to a fixed degree-one polarisation `O_C(1)`). By Riemann--Roch,
764	`χ(C, L ⊗ O_C(n)) = n · deg L + 1 - g`, so the degree is the leading
765	coefficient of `Φ_L(n)`, well-defined on the isomorphism class `[L]` and on
766	the `k`-point `λ` (because `PicScheme C` represents the étale-sheafified
767	relative Picard functor).
768	
769	The degree map is a group homomorphism for the additive structure on
770	`Pic_{C/k}(k)` (tensor product on `L`) and the standard `(ℤ, +)`. The full
771	group-homomorphism refinement / functoriality in `k` lives as a follow-up
772	lemma in iter-186+; the file-skeleton pins only the underlying function.
773	
774	iter-186+: the body extracts the representing invertible sheaf from
775	`(PicScheme.representable C)`, forms its Hilbert polynomial via the project's
776	Hilbert-polynomial machinery (sibling `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`), and returns
777	the leading coefficient. For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed
778	`sorry`. -/
779	noncomputable def degree {k : Type u} [Field k]
780	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
781	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
782	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
783	    (Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left) → ℤ :=
784	  sorry
785	
786	end PicScheme
787	
788	/-! ## §4. `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety
789	
790	The terminal statement of the chapter identifies `Pic⁰_{C/k}` with an
791	abelian variety of dimension `g(C)` --- the Jacobian variety of `C`. In
792	this project, "abelian variety" is the conjunction of the four properties
793	`[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]`
794	threaded through `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity` and consumed by
795	`AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.AlbaneseUP`.
796	
797	Blueprint reference: `thm:pic_zero_is_abelian_variety` (Kleiman §5
798	Ex.~`ex:jac` + Rmk.~`rmk:Jac`; cf. Milne §I.1, Rmk. III.1.4(e)). -/
799	
800	namespace Pic0Scheme
801	
802	/-- **`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety.**
803	
804	For `C/k` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of genus `g = g(C)`
805	over a field `k`, the identity component `Pic⁰_{C/k}` of the Picard scheme
806	is an *abelian variety over* `k`: a smooth, proper, geometrically
807	irreducible `k`-group scheme. (Commutativity follows automatically from
808	Milne §I.1, Cor. 1.4, and is not separately stated here.)
809	
810	The bundled statement: the four pieces `[IsProper] ∧ [Smooth] ∧
811	[GeometricallyIrreducible] ∧ Nonempty (GrpObj _)` hold on
812	`(Pic0Scheme C).hom` and `(Pic0Scheme C)` respectively.
813	
814	Dimension `g = g(C)`: not stated separately at the file-skeleton level
815	(it requires `dim_k (Pic0Scheme C).left = AlgebraicGeometry.genus C`, which
816	involves the Krull-dimension API on the underlying scheme); iter-186+
817	follow-up.
818	
819	iter-186+: the body assembles the four conjuncts from
820	- `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme` (clopen subscheme of
821	  `PicScheme C`),
822	- Kleiman §5 Thm.~`th:qpp&p` (`X/k` projective + geom. integral ⟹
823	  `Pic⁰_{X/k}` quasi-projective; upgrade to projective when geom. normal,
824	  which holds for smooth proper curves of positive genus),
825	- Kleiman §5 Cor.~`cor:sm` + Ex.~`ex:jac` (smoothness of `Pic_{X/k}` at the
826	  identity for smooth proper curves; hence smooth of dimension
827	  `dim_k H¹(C, O_C) = g(C)` everywhere by uniform smoothness on the open
828	  identity component).
829	For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
830	theorem isAbelianVariety {k : Type u} [Field k]
831	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
832	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
833	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
834	    IsProper (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧ Smooth (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
835	      GeometricallyIrreducible (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
836	      Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C)) :=
837	  sorry
838	
839	/-- **Dimension of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` equals the genus of `C`.**
840	
841	Milne~§III.1, Rmk.~1.4(e): "The dimension of `J` is the genus of `C`".
842	For a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C/k` of genus
843	`g = g(C)`, the topological Krull dimension of the underlying scheme of
844	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` equals `g(C)`. By Kleiman~§5 Cor.~`cor:sm`, the inequality
845	`dim Pic_{C/k} ≤ dim_k H¹(C, O_C)` is always an equality at points where
846	`Pic_{C/k}` is smooth, and for smooth proper curves
847	(`SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom`) the identity component is smooth
848	(Kleiman~§5 Ex.~`ex:jac`), so the dimension equals `dim_k H¹(C, O_C) = g(C)`
849	by `def:genus`. -/
850	theorem finrank_eq_genus {k : Type u} [Field k]
851	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
852	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
853	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
854	    topologicalKrullDim (Pic0Scheme C).left = (AlgebraicGeometry.genus C : WithBot ℕ∞) :=
855	  sorry
856	
857	/-- **`k`-points of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` are the kernel of the degree map.**
858	
859	Milne~§III.1, p.~88: `Pic⁰(C)` is the group of isomorphism classes of
860	invertible sheaves of degree zero on `C`. For a smooth proper geometrically
861	integral curve `C/k`, a `k`-point `λ ∈ Pic_{C/k}(k)` lies in the image of
862	the inclusion `Pic⁰_{C/k} ↪ Pic_{C/k}` (the inclusion of
863	`def:pic_zero_subscheme`, packaged here via the existence of the
864	inclusion morphism) if and only if `degree C λ = 0`.
865	
866	The statement-level pin packages two pieces: existence of the inclusion
867	morphism `Pic⁰_{C/k} ⟶ Pic_{C/k}` (extracted from
868	`IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme` once `PicScheme C` has the
869	`GrpObj` + `LocallyOfFiniteType` instances), together with the
870	characterisation of `k`-points factoring through it as those with degree
871	zero. -/
872	theorem kPoints_iff_kerDegree {k : Type u} [Field k]
873	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
874	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
875	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
876	    Nonempty (Σ' (inc : (Pic0Scheme C).left ⟶ (PicScheme C).left),
877	      ∀ (lambda : Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left),
878	        (∃ mu : Spec (.of k) ⟶ (Pic0Scheme C).left, mu ≫ inc = lambda) ↔
879	          PicScheme.degree C lambda = 0) :=
880	  sorry
881	
882	end Pic0Scheme
883	
884	end Scheme
885	
886	end AlgebraicGeometry

185	  haveI : Subsingleton (Spec (.of kbar) : Scheme.{u}) := inferInstance
186	  haveI : Nonempty (Spec (.of kbar) : Scheme.{u}) := inferInstance
187	  -- Step 2: geometric irreducibility over a singleton base gives `IrreducibleSpace`.
188	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace A.left :=

197	/-- **Iter-180 Lane G split (2/2): codim-1-indeterminacy-freeness from
198	Milne's two-input argument.**
199	
200	For a rational map `f : X ⇢ A` from a nonsingular variety `X` to an
201	abelian variety `A` over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every
202	codim-1 point of `X` lies in `f.domain` (`CodimOneFree f`).
203	
204	**Derivation (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, proof of Theorem 3.2, §I.3, p. 17).**
205	Two project-side inputs combine to force the indeterminacy locus empty:
206	
207	1. `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Milne Lemma 3.3 in
208	   `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`): for a rational map from a smooth
209	   integral variety to a *group* variety, the indeterminacy locus is
210	   either empty or pure codim 1.
211	2. The *codim-≥2 conclusion* of Milne Theorem 3.1 (the unbundled half of
212	   `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`):
213	   for a rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety,
214	   the indeterminacy locus has codim `≥ 2`.
215	
216	Combining (1) ∨ (2): pure codim 1 contradicts codim `≥ 2`, so the
217	indeterminacy locus is empty; trivially every point — and in particular
218	every codim-1 point — lies in `f.domain`, so `CodimOneFree f`.
219	
220	**Status of the substantive content.** The codim-≥2 conclusion of
221	Milne 3.1 is not currently exposed by the project as a standalone lemma;
222	it is encapsulated inside `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`'s body (which
223	is itself `sorry` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`). Once the project
224	exposes a lemma `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` separating
225	out the codim-≥2 conclusion, this helper closes via the two-line
226	combination above. Until then it is left as a named `sorry` whose
227	docstring exhaustively records the combination strategy, replacing the
228	iter-179 conjunction-level `sorry` with a more targeted one.
229	
230	This is the iter-180 Lane G refactor's split: the `CodimOneFree f`
231	conjunct is split off into a self-contained named lemma with the
232	substantive missing content localised here. -/
233	private theorem av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy
234	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
235	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
236	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
237	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
238	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
239	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
240	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
241	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
242	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
243	    CodimOneFree f := by
244	  -- **Iter-182 structural refactor.** Materialise the variety-package
245	  -- instances on `A.left` needed by Milne Lemma 3.3
246	  -- (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`): `IsIntegral` from the
247	  -- split helper `av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred`; `IsReduced` from
248	  -- `isReduced_of_isIntegral`; `IsSeparated` from `IsProper`;
249	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType` from `Smooth`.
250	  haveI : IsIntegral A.left := av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred A
251	  haveI : IsReduced A.left := inferInstance
252	  haveI : IsSeparated A.hom := inferInstance
253	  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom := inferInstance
254	  -- Apply Milne Lemma 3.3: the indeterminacy locus is empty or every point
255	  -- of it lies in the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
256	  have hdisj := indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme f
257	  intro x hx
258	  rcases hdisj with hempty | hpure
259	  · -- **Branch 1 (CLOSED).** Empty indeterminacy locus: every point of `X`
260	    -- — in particular `x` — lies in `f.domain` (no codim-1 obstruction).
261	    by_contra hnotin
262	    have hxZ : x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f := hnotin
263	    rw [hempty] at hxZ
264	    exact hxZ.elim
265	  · -- **Branch 2 (PROJECT GAP).** The disjunct gives, for any `x ∈ Z(f)`,
266	    -- a codim-1 generic point `z` with `x ∈ closure {z}`. To prove
267	    -- `x ∈ f.domain` for our codim-1 `x`, contrapositive: assume
268	    -- `x ∈ Z(f)`. The disjunct produces a `z` with `coheight z = 1` and
269	    -- `x ∈ closure {z}` — but for a codim-1 `x`, sobriety of the scheme
270	    -- forces `z = x` (chains of length 1 starting at `z` and ending at a
271	    -- specialisation `x` of equal coheight collapse in a sober T₀ space),
272	    -- so `x = z` is a codim-1 point of `Z(f)`. The contradiction needs
273	    -- the **codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1** (project-side
274	    -- `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`'s unbundled half), which is
275	    -- currently encapsulated inside that theorem's `sorry`'d body in
276	    -- `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` rather than exposed as a separate
277	    -- lemma. Once a lemma
278	    -- `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete : ∀ z ∈ indeterminacyLocus f, coheight z ≥ 2`
279	    -- lands in `CodimOneExtension.lean`, branch 2 closes via:

6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Relative spectrum of a quasi-coherent sheaf of algebras (A.1.a)
10	
11	This file is the **A.1.a** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the relative-spectrum
13	functor `Spec_X(𝒜) : QcohAlg(X)^op ⥤ Sch/X` used by the relative Picard functor on a
14	product `C ×_k T`.
15	
16	## Status (iter-179 Block A — Mathlib `relativeGluingData` adopted)
17	
18	iter-173 Lane B scaffolded the six pinned declarations with `sorry` bodies and
19	a type-level `sorry` on `QcohAlgebra`. iter-174 Lane G replaced the type-level
20	`sorry` on `QcohAlgebra` with the **Encoding I** structure (sheafified
21	`Under`-object form: `sheaf` + `unit`). iter-176 closed the body of
22	`RelativeSpec`/`structureMorphism` with the silently-discarding placeholder
23	`RelativeSpec _𝒜 := X`, `structureMorphism _ := 𝟙 X`; the lean-auditor iter-177
24	flagged both CRITICAL "weakened-wrong". The iter-178 mathlib-analogist consult
25	(`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`) identified that **Mathlib already ships
26	the construction** under a different name —
27	`Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData`
28	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`).
29	
30	**iter-179 Block A** lands the carrier upgrade: `QcohAlgebra` gains the
31	third `coequifibered` field (Stacks 01LL form of quasi-coherence — strictly
32	weaker than `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` and provably equivalent under
33	the dense-subsite equivalence `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`), and the bodies
34	of `RelativeSpec` and `RelativeSpec.structureMorphism` are now the Mathlib
35	canonical values `(relativeGluingData _).glued` and `.toBase`. Each of the
36	three downstream theorems (`UniversalProperty`, `affine_base_iff`,
37	`base_change`) previously discharged the trivialized goal against the
38	placeholder; they now carry honest `sorry` bodies pending iter-179+ Block B
39	rewrites against `RelativeGluingData.cover` /
40	`HasAffineProperty.iff_of_iSup_eq_top` /
41	`SmallAffineZariski.isColimitCocone` (see consult Block B).
42	
43	The 6 pinned declarations are:
44	
45	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QcohAlgebra` (**structure**, Block A iter-179)
46	   — a sheaf of commutative rings on `X`, an `O_X`-algebra unit
47	   `X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf`, and the Stacks-01LL `Coequifibered` overlay consumed
48	   by `relativeGluingData`. See `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
49	   Decision 2.
50	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec` (noncomputable def, ~3 LOC body)
51	   — the relative-spectrum scheme,
52	   `(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`.
53	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty` (theorem, ~15 LOC)
54	   — the structure morphism `Spec_X(𝒜) → X` is an affine morphism; this is the
55	     substantive consequence of the representability statement of Stacks 01LQ.
56	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.affine_base_iff` (theorem, ~8 LOC)
57	   — when `X = Spec R` the relative spectrum is affine (Stacks 01LO).
58	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.base_change` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
59	   — `RelativeSpec` commutes with base change (Stacks 01LS).
60	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.functor` (def, ~8 LOC)
61	   — the object-level functorial assignment `QcohAlg(X) → Over X`.
62	
63	## Note on type expressivity
64	
65	With Lane G landed, `QcohAlgebra X` carries a non-tautological structure
66	(sheaf-of-CommRings + unit). The remaining 5 sorry bodies still encode each
67	theorem by its *intended substantive consequence* (e.g. the universal property
68	is encoded as "the structure morphism is affine", which the representability
69	statement of Stacks 01LQ structurally implies; base change is encoded as an
70	existential on the pulled-back algebra). Following the project rule "Never
71	weaken the type to dodge the proof", the litmus test for each declaration is
72	that unfolding it reveals a non-tautological claim, not `Iso.refl _` or
73	`trivial`. iter-175+ will refine `UniversalProperty` to a
74	`CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy` witness once the
75	`O_X`-algebra Hom-set is wired up via the under-category form
76	`Under X.sheaf ⊆ TopCat.Sheaf CommRingCat X`.
77	
78	## References
79	
80	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_RelativeSpec.tex` (450 LOC, 6 pins).
81	Stacks Project, tags 01LL (situation), 01LO (affine-base case), 01LQ (existence +
82	universal property), 01LR (definition + functoriality), 01LS (base change).
83	Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, II Exercise 5.17.
84	-/
85	
86	set_option autoImplicit false
87	
88	universe u
89	
90	open CategoryTheory Limits
91	
92	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
93	
94	namespace Scheme
95	
96	/-! ## §1. Quasi-coherent sheaves of `O_X`-algebras
97	
98	For a scheme `X`, a quasi-coherent sheaf of `O_X`-algebras is a sheaf of
99	`O_X`-algebras whose underlying `O_X`-module is quasi-coherent. Stacks tag
100	01LL packages the notion as a sheaf $\mathcal{A}$ on $X$ taking values in

300	/-- **Affine-base reduction of the relative spectrum.**
301	
302	For an affine scheme `X = Spec R` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`,
303	the relative spectrum `Spec_X(𝒜)` is itself an affine scheme. (More precisely,
304	there is a canonical isomorphism `Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X, 𝒜))`, but extracting
305	`Γ(X, 𝒜) : CommRingCat` requires the unpacked structure of `QcohAlgebra`,
306	which is iter-174+ work.)
307	
308	iter-174+: refine to the full statement
309	`Nonempty ((Spec R).RelativeSpec 𝒜 ≅ Spec (Γ((Spec R), 𝒜)))`
310	once `Γ` for `QcohAlgebra` is in scope. -/
311	theorem affine_base_iff {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (𝒜 : (Spec R).QcohAlgebra) :
312	    IsAffine ((Spec R).RelativeSpec 𝒜) := by
313	  -- Affineness of the relative-spec total space when the base is affine is the
314	  -- standard "affine over affine is affine" consequence: by
315	  -- `UniversalProperty` the structure morphism is `IsAffineHom`, and `Spec R`
316	  -- is itself `IsAffine`, so `isAffine_of_isAffineHom` closes the goal.
317	  have h : IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) := UniversalProperty 𝒜
318	  exact isAffine_of_isAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
319	
320	end RelativeSpec
321	
322	/-! ## §4.5 `QcohAlgebra.pullback` helpers (iter-180 Lane C)
323	
324	Two named helpers feeding the `coequifibered` field of the `QcohAlgebra.pullback`
325	constructor below. Helper budget = 2 per iter-180 plan. -/
326	
327	/-- **Affineness of the structural pullback projection.**
328	
329	Given `g : T ⟶ X` and `𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra`, the projection
330	`q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜) : (T ×_X Spec_X(𝒜)) ⟶ T` is an affine
331	morphism. Combined with `RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty 𝒜` (which exhibits the
332	relative-spec structure morphism as affine) and the fact that affineness is
333	stable under base change (`MorphismProperty.pullback_fst` on `@isAffineHom`),
334	this is purely an instance derivation. -/
335	lemma QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
336	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
337	    IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
338	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) := by
339	  haveI : IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) :=
340	    RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty 𝒜
341	  exact MorphismProperty.pullback_fst _ _ inferInstance
342	
343	/-- **Stacks 01LL Coequifibered overlay for the pushforward of `O_P` along an
344	affine `q : P ⟶ T`** (iter-180 Lane C, Stacks 01LR pushforward compatibility).
345	
346	Per the Mathlib characterization
347	`Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.coequifibered_iff_forall_isLocalizationAway`, the
348	claim reduces to: for every affine open `U ⊆ T` and section `r : Γ(T, U.1)`,
349	the basic-open restriction `q_* O_P (D(r)) = Γ(P, q⁻¹(D(r)))` is the
350	`IsLocalization.Away` of `q.app r` over `Γ(P, q⁻¹U)`. The named helper
351	`pullback_fst_isAffineHom` exhibits `q` as affine, so every preimage
352	`q⁻¹U` of an affine open `U` is itself affine; combined with
353	`Scheme.Hom.preimage_basicOpen` (which identifies `q⁻¹(T.basicOpen r)` with
354	`P.basicOpen (q.app U.1 r)`), `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen`
355	supplies the localization with the desired algebra structure (restriction
356	along the inclusion `q⁻¹(U.basicOpen r) ⊆ q⁻¹U` induced by
357	`q.preimage_mono` on the AffineZariski-site inclusion). -/
358	lemma QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
359	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
360	    NatTrans.Coequifibered ((AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor T).op.whiskerLeft
361	      ({ hom := (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
362	            (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).c } :
363	        T.sheaf ⟶ (TopCat.Sheaf.pushforward CommRingCat.{u}
364	          (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
365	            (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).base).obj
366	          (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g
367	            (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).sheaf).hom) := by
368	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
369	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
370	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
371	  refine AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.coequifibered_iff_forall_isLocalizationAway.mpr
372	    fun U r => ?_
373	  set q := CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
374	  have hqU : IsAffineOpen (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1) := U.2.preimage q
375	  have hle : (U.basicOpen r).toOpens ≤ U.toOpens := T.basicOpen_le r
376	  exact hqU.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen (q.app U.1 r)
377	    (homOfLE (q.preimage_mono hle)) (q.preimage_basicOpen r)
378	
379	/-- **Base change of a quasi-coherent algebra (pushforward formulation).**
380	
381	Given a morphism `g : T ⟶ X` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, the
382	pulled-back qcoh algebra `g^* 𝒜 : T.QcohAlgebra` is realised as the
383	pushforward of the structure sheaf of the relative-spec pullback. The
384	`sheaf` and `unit` fields are concrete (the topological pushforward of the
385	pullback's structure sheaf and the canonical natural transformation `q.c`
386	respectively, where `q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`). The
387	`coequifibered` field is supplied by the named helper
388	`QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered`, which packages the Stacks-01LR
389	pushforward-along-affine-morphism compatibility. -/
390	noncomputable def QcohAlgebra.pullback {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
391	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : T.QcohAlgebra :=
392	  let q : CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ⟶ T :=
393	    CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
394	  { sheaf := (TopCat.Sheaf.pushforward CommRingCat.{u} q.base).obj
395	      (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).sheaf
396	    unit := ⟨q.c⟩
397	    coequifibered := QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered g 𝒜 }
398	
399	namespace RelativeSpec
400	
401	/-! ## §5. Base change
402	
403	The relative spectrum commutes with base change: for a morphism `g : T → X`
404	and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, the pullback `g^* 𝒜` is a
405	quasi-coherent `O_T`-algebra and
406	`T ×_X Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec_T(g^* 𝒜)`.
407	
408	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_base_change` (Stacks 01LS
409	lemma-spec-base-change). -/
410	
411	/-! ### Helpers for `base_change` (iter-179 Lane B + iter-181 Lane D)
412	
413	The base-change theorem packages the canonical iso content into named helpers
414	(`pullback_iso_affine_piece`, `pullback_iso_construction`, and `pullback_iso`,
415	below) and consumes the file-level `Scheme.QcohAlgebra.pullback` constructor
416	declared just above the namespace. -/
417	
418	/-- **Per-affine-open identification for the base-change iso (iter-181 Lane D
419	helper 1, axiom-clean).**
420	
421	For an affine open `U : T.AffineZariskiSite`, the preimage
422	`q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1` under `q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜)` is itself affine
423	(since `q` is `IsAffineHom` by `QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom`). Its
424	canonical iso with `Spec(Γ(pullback, q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1))` (`IsAffineOpen.isoSpec`)
425	matches `Spec((QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).sheaf.val(.op U.1))`
426	**by definitional unfolding** of the pushforward sheaf
427	(`(QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).sheaf := (pushforward q.base).obj (pullback _).sheaf`,
428	so its value at `U` is `(pullback _).sheaf.val(.op (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1))` defeq).
429	
430	This is the per-affine-piece iso feeding into the global `pullback_iso`
431	construction. Body is a single `(U.2.preimage q).isoSpec` invocation. -/
432	noncomputable def pullback_iso_affine_piece {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
433	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) (U : T.AffineZariskiSite) :
434	    ((CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
435	        (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).toScheme ≅
436	      Scheme.Spec.obj (.op ((QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).sheaf.obj.obj (.op U.1))) :=
437	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
438	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
439	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
440	  (U.2.preimage _).isoSpec
441	
442	/-! ### iter-183 Lane D structural decomposition
443	
444	Five helpers feeding `pullback_iso_construction`:
445	
446	* `pullback_iso_affine_piece` (already declared above, axiom-clean) — per-affine
447	  iso `(q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).toScheme ≅ Spec((g^*𝒜)(U))`.
448	* `pullback_cocone` — cocone on `(relativeGluingData _).functor` with point
449	  `pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)` whose components are the `fromSpec` maps
450	  of the pulled-back affine opens.
451	* `pullback_cocone_desc_comp_fst` — the cocone descent composed with the
452	  pullback projection equals the relative-gluing-data structure morphism;
453	  the load-bearing identification that makes the open-cover argument work.
454	* `pullback_iso_desc_isIso` — the cocone descent is an iso, via the local
455	  affine-open argument on `pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`.
456	* `pullback_iso_construction` — final assembly: `asIso desc |>.symm`.
457	-/
458	
459	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
460	/-- **Cocone on `(relativeGluingData _).functor` with point
461	`pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`** (iter-183 Lane D helper 3,
462	axiom-clean modulo naturality unfolding).
463	
464	The components are the `IsAffineOpen.fromSpec` maps of the pulled-back affine
465	opens `q⁻¹U.1`. Naturality follows from `IsAffineOpen.map_fromSpec` once
466	the relative-gluing-data functor's `map` action is unfolded as
467	`Spec.map (P.presheaf.map ((q.preimage_mono ...).op))`. The unfolding chase
468	is intricate (deep definitional unfolding of `pushforward` and `rightOp`);
469	deferred to iter-184+ as the only remaining work for axiom-clean closure
470	of the entire base-change iso. -/
471	noncomputable def pullback_cocone {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
472	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
473	    haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
474	        (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
475	      QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
476	    Limits.Cocone (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
477	      (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).functor :=
478	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
479	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
480	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
481	  { pt := CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
482	    ι :=
483	    { app := fun U => (U.2.preimage (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
484	        (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜))).fromSpec
485	      naturality := fun U V x => by
486	        -- After unfolding `relativeGluingData.functor.map` through its
487	        -- `rightOp ⋙ Spec` template and the pushforward sheaf, the goal
488	        -- becomes `Spec.map ((pullback _ _).presheaf.map _) ≫
489	        --   (V.2.preimage q).fromSpec = (U.2.preimage q).fromSpec ≫ 𝟙 _`,
490	        -- which is `IsAffineOpen.map_fromSpec` + `Category.comp_id`.
491	        set q := CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
492	          (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
493	        simp only [AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData, Functor.comp_obj,
494	          Functor.comp_map, Functor.rightOp_map, Functor.const_obj_obj,
495	          Functor.const_obj_map]
496	        rw [Category.comp_id]
497	        exact (V.2.preimage q).map_fromSpec (U.2.preimage q) _ } }
498	
499	/-- **The descent composed with `q` equals `d.toBase`** (iter-183 Lane D
500	helper 4, axiom-clean).
501	
502	The pullback-cocone descent `T.RelativeSpec(g^*𝒜) ⟶ pullback g _`, composed
503	with the pullback projection `q := pullback.fst _ _`, equals the relative-
504	gluing-data's structure morphism `d.toBase`. Proof: `colimit.hom_ext`, then
505	the per-component identity uses `SpecMap_appLE_fromSpec`. -/
506	lemma pullback_cocone_desc_comp_fst {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
507	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
508	    haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
509	        (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
510	      QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
511	    haveI : ((AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
512	        (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).functor ⋙
513	        Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected :=
514	      Cover.RelativeGluingData.instIsLocallyDirectedI₀CompFunctorForgetOfIsThin _
515	    Limits.colimit.desc _ (pullback_cocone g 𝒜) ≫
516	        CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) =
517	      (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
518	        (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).toBase := by
519	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
520	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
521	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
522	  haveI : ((AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
523	      (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).functor ⋙
524	      Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected :=
525	    Cover.RelativeGluingData.instIsLocallyDirectedI₀CompFunctorForgetOfIsThin _
526	  set q := CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
527	  set d := AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered
528	  refine Limits.colimit.hom_ext fun U => ?_
529	  rw [← Category.assoc, Limits.colimit.ι_desc, d.ι_toBase U]
530	  change (U.2.preimage q).fromSpec ≫ q = _
531	  rw [← U.2.SpecMap_appLE_fromSpec q (U.2.preimage q) le_rfl,
532	    Scheme.Hom.appLE_eq_app, ← IsAffineOpen.isoSpec_inv_ι, ← Category.assoc]
533	  rfl
534	
535	/-- **The descent map is an isomorphism** (iter-183 Lane D helper 5,
536	substantive — axiom-clean modulo upstream `pullback_cocone_desc_comp_fst`
537	and per-piece restrict-iso identification).
538	
539	The canonical descent map `T.RelativeSpec(g^*𝒜) ⟶ pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`
540	defined by the cocone `pullback_cocone g 𝒜` is an isomorphism. Proof:
541	`IsZariskiLocalAtTarget` for the isomorphism property applied to the
542	affine open cover `{q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1}` of `pullback g _`. The preimage of each
543	piece under `desc` matches `(colimit.ι d.functor U).opensRange` by
544	`pullback_cocone_desc_comp_fst` + `Cover.RelativeGluingData.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι`,
545	and the local restriction is then a composition of three explicit isos. -/
546	lemma pullback_iso_desc_isIso {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
547	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
548	    haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
549	        (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
550	      QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
551	    haveI : ((AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
552	        (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).functor ⋙
553	        Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected :=
554	      Cover.RelativeGluingData.instIsLocallyDirectedI₀CompFunctorForgetOfIsThin _
555	    IsIso (Limits.colimit.desc _ (pullback_cocone g 𝒜)) := by
556	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
557	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
558	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
559	  haveI : ((AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
560	      (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).functor ⋙
561	      Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected :=
562	    Cover.RelativeGluingData.instIsLocallyDirectedI₀CompFunctorForgetOfIsThin _
563	  set q := CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
564	  set d := AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered
565	  set desc := Limits.colimit.desc d.functor (pullback_cocone g 𝒜) with desc_def
566	  refine (IsZariskiLocalAtTarget.iff_of_iSup_eq_top
567	    (P := MorphismProperty.isomorphisms Scheme)
568	    (fun U : T.AffineZariskiSite => q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1) ?_).mpr fun U ↦ ?_
569	  · -- iSup of preimages = preimage of iSup = preimage of ⊤ = ⊤.
570	    rw [← Scheme.Hom.preimage_iSup]
571	    convert Scheme.Hom.preimage_top q using 2
572	    exact iSup_affineOpens_eq_top T
573	  · -- Per-piece: `desc ∣_ q⁻¹U.1` is iso. Following the template at
574	    -- `SmallAffineZariski.isColimitCocone` (file
575	    -- `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:343`), reduce
576	    -- to `IsIso (pullback.snd desc (q⁻¹U.1).ι)` and build an iso
577	    -- `pullback desc (q⁻¹U.1).ι ≅ d.functor.obj U` via range matching using
578	    -- the key fact `hPre`.
579	    have hPre : desc ⁻¹ᵁ q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1 = (Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).opensRange := by
580	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_preimage, pullback_cocone_desc_comp_fst g 𝒜]
581	      have h := d.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι U
582	      simpa [AffineZariskiSite.directedCover, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι] using h
583	    -- The per-piece factorisation: `desc ∣_ q⁻¹U.1` factors as three isos:
584	    --   (1) `Scheme.isoOfEq _ hPre`:
585	    --       `(desc⁻¹q⁻¹U.1).toScheme ≅ (colim.ι U).opensRange.toScheme`
586	    --   (2) `colim.ι U .isoOpensRange.symm`:
587	    --       `(colim.ι U).opensRange.toScheme ≅ d.functor.obj U`
588	    --   (3) `(pullback_iso_affine_piece g 𝒜 U).symm`:
589	    --       `d.functor.obj U ≅ Spec((g^*𝒜)(U)) ≅ (q⁻¹U.1).toScheme`
590	    --       (the inverse via `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec.inv ≫ ι`).
591	    -- Commute the chain through `(q⁻¹U.1).ι` by canceling the mono ι.
592	    let iso_chain : (desc ⁻¹ᵁ q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).toScheme ≅ (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).toScheme :=
593	      Scheme.isoOfEq _ hPre ≪≫
594	        (Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).isoOpensRange.symm ≪≫
595	        (pullback_iso_affine_piece g 𝒜 U).symm
596	    suffices h : desc ∣_ q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1 = iso_chain.hom by
597	      change IsIso (desc ∣_ q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1)
598	      rw [h]; exact iso_chain.isIso_hom
599	    -- Verify equality by post-composing with `(q⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι` (which is mono).

205	The first sub-lemma packages the construction and elementary regularity
206	of the Abel–Jacobi morphism `ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` at the basepoint
207	`P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`. On `k̄`-points,
208	`ι_{P₀}(Q) = [𝓞_C(Q − P₀)] ∈ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}(k̄)`; at the basepoint
209	itself `ι_{P₀}(P₀) = η_J`.
210	
211	This is the content gated on A.3 (existence of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` as a
212	representable group scheme); the morphism is the moduli classifier of
213	the rigidified diagonal correspondence
214	`𝓛^{P₀} = 𝓞_{C × C}(Δ − {P₀} × C − C × {P₀})` on `C × C`, taken as a
215	relative degree-zero line bundle on `C × C` over the second factor.
216	
217	Blueprint reference: `lem:abel_jacobi_morphism` (Milne, *Abelian
218	Varieties*, Proposition III.6.1 and Summary III.6.11). -/
